_about: Raw corpus for the anthem-belligerence calibration sample (~10 anthems chosen to stress every part of the rubric). 'key_lines' are evidence phrases drawn verbatim from the cited sources; full verbatim lyrics are NOT stored here (public-domain texts live at the source URLs). Each anthem has a 'completeness' note recording whether ALL verses were reviewed for scoring. Scores in data/processed/scores.json are grounded in these lines + the cited sources. Retrieved 2026-05-28 via web search; verse-completeness re-audited the same day.
_completeness_policy: as-written = the complete OFFICIAL lyrics. Non-official historical verses (e.g. the UK 'Marshal Wade / rebellious Scots' verse) are documented but EXCLUDED from as-written scoring. Where not every verse was individually captured, the 'completeness' field says so and notes whether the score is robust regardless.

anthems.title: La Marseillaise
anthems.sung_rule: Customarily verse 1 + refrain (sometimes also verse 6). All verses official.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: v1+refrain and v6 captured; verses 2-5 and 7 not individually transcribed but are all known to sustain martial/blood imagery. as-written belligerence is at/near the ceiling on v1 alone, so the missing verses cannot lower it.

as_sung_v1_refrain: Arise, children of the Fatherland, the day of glory has arrived!
Against us tyranny's bloody banner is raised
ferocious soldiers ... come to cut the throats of your sons, your companions
To arms, citizens, form your battalions
Let an impure blood water our furrows
as_written_extra: v6: Sacred love of the Fatherland
verses 2-5,7 sustain martial/blood imagery (not individually transcribed)

anthems.title: The Star-Spangled Banner
anthems.sung_rule: Verse 1 only is performed in virtually all settings.

as_sung_v1: the rockets' red glare, the bombs bursting in air
gave proof through the night that our flag was still there
the land of the free and the home of the brave
as_written_extra: v2: Where the foe's haughty host in dread silence reposes ... 'Tis the star-spangled banner, in full glory reflected
v3: No refuge could save the hireling and slave / From the terror of flight or the gloom of the grave
v4: Then conquer we must, when our cause it is just
v4: And this be our motto: 'In God is our trust'
v4: the land of the free and the home of the brave (freemen)

anthems.title: God Save the King
anthems.structure: Standard official text is 3 verses (no single legally-fixed version).
anthems.sung_rule: Verse 1 alone is sung even on official occasions; occasionally + a later verse. The anti-enemy verse 2 is rarely performed.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE for the official text (v1-3). v2 carries the belligerent content; v3 is benign ('defend our laws'). The famous 'rebellious Scots to crush' verse is NON-OFFICIAL (see below) and excluded from as-written.

as_sung_v1: Send him victorious, happy and glorious
as_written_extra: v2: O Lord our God arise, Scatter our enemies, And make them fall
v2: Confound their politics, Frustrate their knavish tricks
v3 (standard): Thy choicest gifts in store on him be pleased to pour ... May he defend our laws (benign)
key_lines.non_official_historical_verse: c.1745 anti-Jacobite verse, NEVER in the official published version and out of regular use for 100+ years: 'May he sedition hush, And like a torrent rush, Rebellious Scots to crush'. Documented here but EXCLUDED from as-written scoring per the completeness policy.

anthems.title: Kassaman (We Pledge)
anthems.sung_rule: All five verses are officially part of the anthem (full performance mandated since 2008). The anti-France verse 3 was historically sometimes omitted.

full: We swear by the lightning that destroys, by the streams of generous blood being shed
the bright flags that wave ... on the high djebels
that we are in revolt, whether to live or to die
We are soldiers in revolt for truth ... we have fought for our independence
the noise of gunpowder as our rhythm and the sound of machine guns as our melody
O France, this is the day of reckoning, so prepare to receive from us our answer
From our heroes we shall make an army ... from our dead we shall build up a glory
Let it be written with the blood of martyrs

anthems.title: Kimigayo
anthems.sung_rule: Single verse; as-written = as-sung.

full: May your reign continue for a thousand, eight thousand generations
until the tiny pebbles grow into massive boulders lush with moss

anthems.notes: Title ~ 'His Imperial Majesty's Reign'; about the longevity of the Emperor's reign.

anthems.title: Swiss Psalm
anthems.sung_rule: Verse 1 most commonly sung; all official. Deity + nature appear in all verses incl. v1, so little gap.

full: When the morning skies grow red ... Thou, O Lord, appeareth in their light
When the alps glow bright with splendour, pray to God, to Him surrender
that God, the Lord, dwelleth in this land
Thou, O loving Father, ever near
When dark clouds enshroud the hills and grey mist the valley fills

anthems.title: Marcha Real
anthems.sung_rule: Wordless since 1978; one of only a few anthems with no official lyrics.
anthems.completeness: N/A: wordless anthem, nothing to capture.
anthems.notes: Edge case: scored 0 on all themes, flagged no-lyrics, excluded from index distributions (reported separately).

anthems.title: Deutschlandlied (Das Lied der Deutschen)
anthems.sung_rule: ONLY the 3rd stanza is the official anthem / sung. Stanzas 1 and 2 are not performed.

as_sung_st3: Unity and justice and freedom for the German fatherland
Flourish in the radiance of this happiness, flourish, German fatherland
as_written_extra: st1: Germany, Germany above everything, above everything in the world
st1: From the Meuse to the Neman, from the Adige to the Belt (boundaries)
st2: German women, German loyalty, German wine and German songs

anthems.notes: st2 ('German women ... German wine and German songs') is the wildcard-theme test case.

anthems.title: Sayaun Thunga Phulka
anthems.sung_rule: Whole anthem sung; as-written = as-sung.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: full two-stanza English translation captured (corrected from an earlier theme-summary that missed the 'blood of the braves' line).

full: Woven from hundreds of flowers, we are one garland that's Nepali
Spread sovereign from Mechi to Mahakali
A shawl of nature's wealth unending
From the blood of the braves, a nation free and non-moving
A land of knowledge, of peace, the plains, hills and mountains tall
Indivisible, this beloved land of ours, our motherland Nepal
Of many races, languages, religions, and cultures of incredible sprawl
This progressive nation of ours, all hail Nepal!

anthems.title: National Anthem of South Africa
anthems.structure: Hybrid combining 'Nkosi Sikelel' iAfrika' + 'Die Stem'; 5 languages
anthems.sung_rule: One combined song, sung in full; as-written = as-sung.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: all five language sections captured (isiXhosa/isiZulu/Sesotho/Afrikaans/English).

full: Nkosi Sikelel' iAfrika - Lord/God bless Africa
Banish wars and strife
Lord, bless our nation of South Africa
Sounds the call to come together, and united we shall stand
Let us live and strive for freedom
over our everlasting mountains

anthems.notes: Calibration trap: 'Banish wars and strife' mentions war but as a plea to END it - must NOT score as belligerent.

anthems.title: Angola Avante (Forward Angola)
anthems.sung_rule: Both verses + chorus are official; the latter third of each verse functions as refrain, chorus repeated on single-verse renditions. Treated as-sung = full anthem (both verses + chorus). Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Oh Fatherland, we shall never forget the heroes of the Fourth of February.
Oh Fatherland, we salute your children who died for our Independence.
We honour the past and our History, as by our work we build the New Man.
Forward, Angola! Revolution through the power of the People: A United Country, Freedom, One People, one Nation!
Let us raise our liberated voices for the glory of the peoples of Africa.
We shall march, Angolan fighters, in solidarity with oppressed peoples.
We shall fight proudly for Peace along with the progressive forces of the world.

anthems.title: L'Aube Nouvelle (The New Dawn)
anthems.sung_rule: First verse and chorus usually performed at official occasions (per nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia). as-written = chorus + all 3 verses; as-sung = chorus + verse 1.

evidence: Children of Benin, arise! The resounding cry of freedom Is heard at the first light of dawn; Children of Benin, arise!
Formerly, at her call, our ancestors Knew how to engage in mighty battles With strength, courage, ardour, and full of joy, but at the price of blood.
Builders of the present, you too, join forces Each day for the task stronger in unity.
When all around there blows a wind of anger and hate: Citizen of Benin, be proud, and in a calm spirit Trusting in the future, behold your flag!
The red signifies the courage of your ancestors; The yellow foretells the richest treasures.
Beloved Benin, your sunny mountains, palm trees, and green pastures Show everywhere your brightness; Your soil offers everyone the richest fruits.
Benin, from henceforth your sons are united With one brotherly spirit
original: Enfants du Bénin debout! La liberté d'un cri sonore Chante aux premiers feux de l'aurore; Enfants du Bénin debout!
Jadis à son appel, nos aieux sans faiblesse Ont su avec courage, ardeur pleins d'allegresse Livrer au prix du sang des combats éclatants.

anthems.title: Fatshe leno la rona (Blessed Be This Noble Land / Our Land)
anthems.sung_rule: Usual rendition is verse 1 + chorus; verse 2 + chorus may also be performed (per nationalanthems.info). as-written = both verses + chorus; as-sung = verse 1 + chorus.

evidence: This land of ours, Is a gift from God, An inheritance from our forefathers; May it always be at peace.
Awake, awake, O men, awake! Arise, O women, arise! Be energized, Let us work together to serve, Our land.
Beautiful name of fame Of the nation of Botswana Through harmonious relations and reconciliation Bound together
original: Fatshe leno la rona Ke mpho ya Modimo, Ke boswa jwa borraetsho; a le nne ka kagiso.

anthems.title: Le Ditanyè (Une Seule Nuit / One Single Night; L'Hymne de la victoire)
anthems.structure: multiple verses + chorus (chorus + verse 1 = official short version)
anthems.sung_rule: First verse and chorus considered the official lyrics and performed at most official occasions (per nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia). as-written = full revolutionary text (incl. later verses); as-sung = chorus + verse 1.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: chorus + verse 1 fully captured; one later verse captured (the '4 August volunteers' verse); some intermediate verse lines only summarized in sources

evidence: And one single night has drawn together The history of an entire people, And one single night has launched its triumphal march. Towards the horizon of good fortune.
One single night has brought together our people With all the peoples of the World, In the acquisition of liberty and progress.
Motherland or death, we shall conquer.

Nourished in the lively source of the Revolution, The volunteers for liberty and peace With their nocturnal and beneficial energies of the 4 of August Had not only hand arms, but also and above all The flame in their hearts lawfully to free Faso forever from the fetters of those who Here and there were polluting the sacred soul of independence and sovereignty.
Against the cynical malice transformed into neo-colonialism and its petty local servants

anthems.title: Burundi Bwacu (Our Burundi)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem; all three verses are official and customarily sung as one piece. as-written and as-sung identical. Source: lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Beloved Burundi, gentle country, Take your place in the concert of nations, Acceding to independence with honourable intentions.
Wounded and bruised, you have remained master of yourself. When the hour came, you arose, Lifting yourself proudly into the ranks of free peoples.
Beloved Burundi, sacred heritage from our forefathers, Recognized as worthy of self-government, With your courage you also have a sense of honour. Sing the glory of liberty conquered again.
Beloved Burundi, worthy of our tenderest love, We vow to your noble service our hands and hearts and lives.
May God, who gave you to us, keep you for us to venerate, Under the shield of unity, In peace, joy and prosperity.

anthems.title: Cântico da Liberdade (Song of Freedom)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem performed in full; as-written and as-sung identical. Source: lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: full text reviewed (peace/freedom/sea/island themes; no martial content)

evidence: Sing, brother Sing, my brother For Liberty is a hymn And Man the certainty.
With dignity, bury the seed In the dust of the naked island At the escarpment of life
Hope is as big as the sea Which embraces us Sentinel of the oceans and winds Persevering Between the stars and the Atlantic Intone the Chant of Liberty.

anthems.title: O Cameroon, Cradle of Our Forefathers (Chant de Ralliement / The Rallying Song)
languages_scored: English (official English version by Bernard Nsokika Fonlon)
anthems.sung_rule: Both verses + chorus are official; verse 1 + chorus typically performed at ceremonies. as-written = both verses + chorus; as-sung = verse 1 + chorus.

evidence: O Cameroon, Thou Cradle of our Fathers, Holy Shrine where in our midst they now repose, Their tears and blood and sweat thy soil did water, On thy hills and valleys once their tillage rose.
Dear Fatherland, thy worth no tongue can tell! How can we ever pay thy due? Thy welfare we will win in toil and love and peace, Will be to thy name ever true!
Land of Promise, land of Glory! Thou, of life and joy, our only store! Thine be honour, thine devotion, And deep endearment, for evermore.
From Shari, from where the Mungo meanders From along the banks of lowly Boumba Stream, Muster thy sons in union close around thee, Mighty as the Buea Mountain be their team;
Foster, for Mother Africa, a loyalty That true shall remain to the last.

anthems.title: La Renaissance (The Rebirth; Sango: E Zingo)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem; main verse + refrain performed as one piece. as-written and as-sung identical. Source: lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia.

evidence: Oh! Central Africa, cradle of the Bantu! Take up again your right to respect, to life!
Long subjugated, long scorned by all, But, from today, breaking tyranny's hold.
Through work, order and dignity You reconquer your rights, your unity, And to take this new step The voice of our ancestors calls us.
To work! In order and dignity, In the respect for rights and in unity, Breaking poverty and tyranny, Holding high the flag of the Fatherland.
original: Brisons la misère et la tyrannie

anthems.title: La Tchadienne
anthems.structure: chorus + 4 verses (chorus + verse 1 = official anthem; other 3 verses non-official and barely known)
anthems.sung_rule: Only the chorus and first verse constitute the official national anthem; the other three verses are non-official. as-written = official text (chorus + verse 1); as-sung identical. Non-official verses not scored. Source: nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE for official text (chorus + verse 1); 3 non-official verses excluded by rubric (not located in full anyway)

evidence: People of Chad, arise and take up the task! You have conquered your soil and won your rights;
Your freedom will be born of your courage. Lift up your eyes, the future is yours
Oh, my Country, may God protect you, May your neighbours admire your children.
Joyful, peaceful, advance as you sing, Faithful to your fathers who are watching you.
original: Peuple Tchadien, debout et à l'ouvrage! Tu as conquis ta terre et ton droit;
Ta liberté naîtra de ton courage.
O mon Pays, que Dieu te prenne en garde

anthems.title: Udzima wa ya Masiwa (The Union of the Great Islands)
anthems.sung_rule: Full lyrics presented and performed as one piece; no single-verse custom indicated. as-written and as-sung identical. Source: lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia.

evidence: The flag is flying, Announcing complete independence; The nation rises up Because of the faith we have In this our Comoria.
Let us always have devotion To love our Great Islands.
We Comorians are of one blood, We Comorians are of one faith.
On these Islands we were born, These Islands brought us up.
May God always help us; Let us always have the firm resolve To love our fatherland, Love our religion and the world.

anthems.title: La Congolaise (The Congolese)
anthems.sung_rule: First verse and chorus constitute the official anthem and are most commonly performed; the full written text has 3 verses. as-written = all 3 verses + chorus; as-sung = verse 1 + chorus. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: On this day the sun rises And our Congo stands resplendent. A long night is ended, A great happiness has come. Let us all, with wild joyfulness, sing The song of freedom.
Arise, Congolese, proud every man, Proclaim the unity of our nation. Let us forget what divides us And become more united than ever. Let us live our motto: Unity, work, progress.
From the forest to the bush, From the bush to the ocean, One people, one soul, One heart, ardent and proud. Let us all fight, every one of us, For our old black country.
And if we have to die, What does it really matter? Our children Everywhere will be able to say how Triumph comes through battle, And in the smallest village Sing beneath our three colours.

anthems.title: Debout Congolais (Arise, Congolese)
anthems.structure: choir/opening + verse + refrain + final verse (all official, sung as one piece)
anthems.sung_rule: Sung as one continuous official piece (opening + verse + refrain + final verse); no separate single-verse custom. as-written and as-sung identical. Source: lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia.

evidence: Arise, Congolese, United by fate, United in the struggle for independence, Let us hold our heads high, long bowed,
Oh ardent people, by hard work, We will build a country more beautiful than before, In peace.
Citizens, sing the sacred hymn Of your solidarity, Proudly Greet the golden emblem of your sovereignty, Congo.
Blessed gift, Congo of our ancestors, Oh country, oh Congo Beloved, Congo We will populate your soil And ensure your greatness.
Sacred day of the immortal oath of freedom That we pass on To our posterity Forever.
original: Debout Congolais, Unis par le sort, Unis dans l'effort pour l'indépendance
Don béni, Congo des aïeux, O pays, ô Congo Bien-aimé

anthems.title: Mu isamaa, mu õnn ja rõõm (My Fatherland, My Happiness and Joy)
anthems.sung_rule: All three stanzas are the official text and are typically sung; verse 1 is the most familiar. Treated as-written = all 3 stanzas; as-sung defaulted to verse 1 (sung-assumed) since ceremonial custom of how many stanzas is not firmly fixed. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: My native land, my joy - delight, How fair thou art - how bright!
For nowhere in the world around Can ever such a place be found So well belov'd, from sense profound, My native country dear!
May my last breath be thanks to thee, For true to death I'll ever be, O worthy, most belov'd and fine, Thou, dearest country mine!
May God in Heaven thee defend, My own beloved land! May He be guard, may He be shield, For ever bless and guardian wield.

anthems.title: Maamme / Vårt land (Our Land)
anthems.structure: 11 stanzas (original poem); national anthem use is stanzas 1 and 11
anthems.sung_rule: Customarily only the 1st and last (11th) stanzas are sung; the full poem has 11 stanzas. As-written = all 11 stanzas (content reviewed via summaries); as-sung = stanzas 1 and 11. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: stanzas 1 and 11 retrieved verbatim; intervening stanzas reviewed in summary, no belligerent content surfaced

evidence: Our land, our land, our fatherland, Sound loud, O name of worth!
No mount that meets the heaven's band, No hidden vale, no wavewashed strand, Is loved, as is our native North, Our own forefathers' earth.
Thy blossom, in the bud laid low, Yet ripened shall upspring.
See! From our love once more shall grow Thy light, thy joy, thy hope, thy glow!

anthems.title: Ýmnos is tin Eleftherían (Hymn to Liberty)
anthems.structure: 158 stanzas (original poem); first 2 stanzas are the official anthem
anthems.sung_rule: Only the first two stanzas are the official national anthem and are always sung at ceremonies. As-written and as-sung are IDENTICAL (the official text is the 2 stanzas; the remaining 156 stanzas are non-official and not scored). Source: Wikipedia / Greek Presidency / nationalanthems.info.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both official stanzas reviewed (remaining 156 stanzas of the poem are not the anthem and excluded)

evidence: We knew thee of old, Oh divinely restored, By the light of thine eyes And the light of thy Sword.
From the graves of our slain Shall thy valour prevail As we greet thee again - Hail, Liberty!
I recognize you by the fearsome sharpness of your sword.
From the sacred bones of the Hellenes arisen, and valiant again as you once were, Hail, o hail, Liberty!

anthems.title: Himnusz (Isten, áldd meg a magyart / Hymn)
anthems.sung_rule: All 8 verses are official text; in public only the first verse is performed. As-written = all 8 verses; as-sung = verse 1. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / Classic FM.

evidence: O Lord, bless the nation of Hungary With your grace and bounty Extend toward it your guarding arm During strife with its enemies
This nation has suffered for all sins Of the past and of the future!
Now the plundering Mongols' arrows You swarmed over us Then the Turks' slave yoke We took upon our shoulders.
Over the corpses of our defeated army A victory song!
Sea of blood beneath his feet Ocean of flame above.
Castle stood, now a heap of stones ... Groans of death, weeping Now sound in their place.
Redeem, O Lord, the Hungarians Who are tossed by waves of danger ... On the sea of its misery

anthems.title: Lofsöngur (Ó Guð vors lands / Song of Praise)
anthems.sung_rule: All 3 stanzas are official; only the first stanza is sung on a regular basis. As-written = all 3 stanzas; as-sung = stanza 1. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Our country's God! Our country's God! We worship Thy name in its wonder sublime.
Our God, our God, we bow to Thee, Our spirits most fervent we place in thy care. Lord, God of our fathers from age unto age.
We pray and we thank Thee a thousand years For safely protected we stand.
Inspire us at morn with Thy courage and love, And lead through the days of our strife!
At evening send peace from Thy heaven above, And safeguard our nation through life.

anthems.title: Amhrán na bhFiann (The Soldier's Song)
languages_scored: English (original English text; Irish version sung today)
anthems.structure: 3 verses + chorus; the chorus alone is the official national anthem
anthems.sung_rule: Only the chorus is the official national anthem and is what is performed. As-written and as-sung are IDENTICAL (= the chorus; the 3 verses of the wider song are not the official anthem and are not scored). Source: Wikipedia / Classic FM / nationalanthems.info.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: official chorus reviewed (the song's three verses are not the official anthem and excluded)

evidence: Soldiers are we, Whose lives are pledged to Ireland
Sworn to be free, no more our ancient sireland Shall shelter the despot or the slave;
Tonight we man the Bearna Baoil In Erin's cause come woe or weal,
'Mid cannon's roar and rifle's peal, We'll chant a soldier's song.

anthems.title: Il Canto degli Italiani (Fratelli d'Italia / Inno di Mameli)
anthems.structure: 6 strophes, each followed by the refrain
anthems.sung_rule: All strophes + refrain are official; customarily only strophe 1 (sung twice) + the refrain are performed. As-written = all 6 strophes + refrain; as-sung = strophe 1 + refrain. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: strophe 1 + refrain verbatim; strophes 4 and 5 retrieved verbatim; strophes 2-3 and 6 reviewed in summary

evidence: Fratelli d'Italia, l'Italia s'è desta, dell'elmo di Scipio s'è cinta la testa.
Brothers of Italy, Italy has woken, bound Scipio's helmet Upon her head.
ché schiava di Roma Iddio la creò. (Because as a slave of Rome God created her)
Stringiamci a coorte, siam pronti alla morte. (Let us join in a cohort, we are ready to die.)
Già l'Aquila d'Austria Le penne ha perdute. (Already the Eagle of Austria Has lost its feathers.)
Il sangue d'Italia E il sangue Polacco Bevé, col Cosacco (The blood of Italy, the Polish blood she drank with the Cossack)
Uniamoci, amiamoci, l'unione e l'amore (Let us unite, let us love one another)

anthems.title: Dievs, svētī Latviju! (God Bless Latvia!)
anthems.structure: 2 short stanzas (the second largely repeats lines of the first)
anthems.sung_rule: Short single official text; sung in full. As-written and as-sung are IDENTICAL. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: God bless Latvia, Our beloved fatherland, Bless Latvia, Oh, bless it dearly!
Where Latvian daughters bloom, Where Latvian sons sing, Let us flourish in happiness In our Latvia!

anthems.title: Oben am jungen Rhein (High Above the Young Rhine)
anthems.sung_rule: Two official stanzas; both customarily sung (described as first and last). As-written and as-sung are IDENTICAL. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: High above the young Rhine Lies Liechtenstein, resting On Alpine heights.
This beloved homeland, This dear fatherland Was chosen for us by God's wise hand.
Long live the Prince of the Land, Long live our fatherland,
United by brotherly bonds and free.

anthems.title: Tautiška giesmė (Lietuva, Tėvyne mūsų / National Hymn)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem; all 4 stanzas are official and sung. As-written and as-sung are IDENTICAL. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Lithuania, our dear homeland, Land of worthy heroes! May your sons draw strength From your past experiences.
May your children always Choose the paths of virtue, May they work towards your good, And that of all people.
May the sun of Lithuania Disperse darkness, And light, and truth, May guide our steps.
May love of Lithuania Burn in our hearts, In the name of Lithuania, Unity may blossom!

anthems.title: Ons Heemecht (Our Homeland)
anthems.structure: 4 stanzas (original poem); official anthem = stanzas 1 and 4
anthems.sung_rule: By law (17 June 1993) the official anthem is stanzas 1 and 4 only. As-written = official text = stanzas 1 and 4; as-sung IDENTICAL (both official stanzas performed). Source: luxembourg.public.lu / Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both official stanzas (1 and 4) reviewed; stanzas 2-3 are non-official and excluded

evidence: Where the Alzette flows through the meadows, The Sura breaks through the crags.
Where the vine fragrant grows along the Moselle, The sky promises us wine:
There lies the land for which we willingly Dare everything down here. Our homeland which we so deeply Carry in our hearts.
O Thou above, Whose powerful hand Leads the nations through the world, Protect the Luxembourg land From foreign yoke and threat.
As children Thou already instilled in us all The spirit of freedom.

anthems.title: L-Innu Malti (The Maltese Hymn)
anthems.structure: 1 stanza of 6 lines (a single short prayer)
anthems.sung_rule: Short single official text, sung in full. As-written and as-sung are IDENTICAL. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Guard her, O Lord, as ever Thou hast guarded! This Motherland so dear whose name we bear!
Keep her in mind, whom Thou hast made so fair!
May he who rules, for wisdom be regarded! In master mercy, strength in man increase!
Confirm us all, in unity and peace!

anthems.title: Limba noastră (Our Language)
anthems.sung_rule: The officially adopted anthem (since 1994/95) is exactly these 5 stanzas; the whole short anthem is performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia 'Limba noastră', nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.

evidence: Our language is a treasure that surges from deep shadows of the past, Chain of precious stones that scattered all over our ancient land.
Our language is a fire that burns In a people who without warning Awoke from the sleep of death Like the hero from the tale.
Our language is the greenest leaf of the everlasting codrii, the gentle Nistru's ripple.
Our language is a holy tongue, The tongue of ancient missals, which the peasants weep and sing by their hearths.

anthems.title: Hymne Monégasque / A Marcia de Muneghu (The March of Monaco)
anthems.structure: 1 official verse (Monégasque lyrics by Louis Notari)
anthems.sung_rule: Only the single Notari Monégasque verse is official; only it is ever sung (usually instrumental). as-written = as-sung. A separate 1841 French text by Castinelli is historical/non-official and is NOT scored (it was far more martial: 'We shall all die in his defence, but after us, our children will fight'). Source: Wikipedia 'Hymne monégasque', nationalanthems.info, Prince's Palace site.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: the single official Monégasque verse reviewed (non-official 1841 French verses noted but not scored)

evidence: Forever, in our land, One flag has flown in the wind
Forever, the colours red and white Have symbolised our liberty
We celebrate the same religion; We have the honour To have always had the same Princes.
And nothing will change As long as the sun shines; God will always help us

anthems.title: Oj, svijetla majska zoro (Oh, Bright Dawn of May)
anthems.structure: 4 stanzas as adopted (two distinct quatrains, each framed by the repeated 'Oj svijetla majska zoro / Majko naša Crna Goro' couplet)
anthems.sung_rule: The whole short adopted anthem (all four stanzas) is performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia 'Oj, svijetla majska zoro', nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.

evidence: Oh the bright dawn of May, Montenegro our mother
We are sons of your rocks and keepers of your honesty
We love you, the rocky hills and their eerie gorges deep that had never to endure the shameful chains of slavery.
While our unity gives wings to our Lovcen cause, Proud shall be, celebrated will be Our dear homeland
A river of our waves, jumping into two seas, will bear voice to the ocean: May eternal be our Montenegro!

anthems.title: Wilhelmus (Wilhelmus van Nassouwe)
anthems.structure: 15 verses (acrostic spelling WILLEM VAN NASSOV); written 16th c.
anthems.sung_rule: as-written = the full 15-verse official text. as-sung = verse 1 (and on official occasions verse 6, 'My shield and reliance'); these are the only verses customarily performed. Source: Royal House of the Netherlands site, Wikipedia 'Wilhelmus', nationalanthems.info.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: verses 1 and 6 reviewed verbatim (the sung verses); the remaining 13 written verses summarised from sources (William's exile, faith, appeals to God and to subjects) but not all quoted line-by-line

evidence: William of Nassau, scion Of a Dutch and ancient line, I dedicate undying Faith to this land of mine.
A prince I am, undaunted, Of Orange, ever free, To the king of Spain I've granted A lifelong loyalty.
I've ever tried to live in The fear of God's command
My shield and reliance are you, o God my Lord. It is you on whom I want to rely, never leave me again.
Grant that I may remain brave, your servant for always, and may defeat the tyranny, which pierces my heart.

anthems.title: Denes nad Makedonija (Today over Macedonia)
anthems.structure: 4 stanzas (each with a repeated final couplet); stanza 2 often omitted on government texts
anthems.sung_rule: as-written = all 4 official stanzas. as-sung = stanzas 1, 3, 4 (stanza 2, naming the revolutionaries, is frequently omitted in official/performed versions). Source: Wikipedia 'Denes nad Makedonija', nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.

evidence: Today over Macedonia A new sun of freedom rises, Macedonians fight For their rights.
Now once again flies The flag of the Krusevo Republic, Goce Delcev, Pitu Guli, Dame Gruev, Sandanski.
The Macedonian woodlands sing brightly New songs, new awakenings. Free Macedonia Lives free.
Don't cry Macedonia, my dear mother, Hold your head up high, Macedonia is free, and free it shall live!

anthems.title: Ja, vi elsker dette landet (Yes, We Love This Country)
anthems.sung_rule: as-written = all 8 verses. as-sung = verses 1, 7, 8 (the customarily performed verses; verse 1 by far most common). Source: Wikipedia 'Ja, vi elsker dette landet', nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: verses 1, 7, 8 (the sung verses) reviewed verbatim; the intervening verses summarised from sources (love of the land, history, hardship) but not all quoted line-by-line

evidence: Yes, we love with fond devotion This our land that looms Rugged, storm-scarred o'er the ocean With her thousand homes.
Norwegian man in house and cabin, thank your great God! The country he wanted to protect, although things looked dark.
All the fights fathers have fought, and the mothers have wept, the Lord has quietly eased so we won our rights.
And as fathers' fight has lifted it from need to victory, also we, when it is required, for its peace shall camp.

anthems.title: Mazurek Dąbrowskiego (Dąbrowski's Mazurka / Poland Is Not Yet Lost)
anthems.structure: 4 verses + recurring chorus (official since 1927; the original Wybicki poem had more stanzas, only 4 are official)
anthems.sung_rule: All 4 official verses + chorus are performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia 'Poland Is Not Yet Lost', nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: all 4 official verses + chorus reviewed

evidence: Poland has not yet perished, So long as we still live. What the foreign force has taken from us, We shall with sabre retrieve.
March, march, Dąbrowski, From Italy to Poland. Under your command We shall rejoin the nation.
We'll cross the Vistula, we'll cross the Warta, We shall be Polish. Bonaparte has given us the example Of how we should prevail.
Like Czarniecki to Poznań After the Swedish annexation, To save our homeland, We shall return across the sea.
A father, in tears, Says to his Basia Listen, our boys are said To be beating the tarabans.

anthems.title: A Portuguesa (The Portuguese)
anthems.structure: 3 verses + chorus; official version = verse 1 + chorus only
anthems.sung_rule: The official anthem (1957) is verse 1 + the chorus; that is what is performed. as-written = as-sung (verses 2 and 3 are non-official and NOT scored, though they continue maritime/'gave new worlds' imagery). Source: Wikipedia 'A Portuguesa', nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: the official verse 1 + chorus reviewed (non-official verses 2-3 noted but not scored)

evidence: Heroes of the sea, noble people, Valiant and immortal nation, Raise once again today The splendor of Portugal!
Of your distinguished forefathers, That shall lead you on to victory!
To arms, to arms! Over land, over sea, To arms, to arms! For the Fatherland, fight!
Against the cannons, march on, march on!

anthems.title: Deșteaptă-te, române! (Awaken, Romanian!)
anthems.sung_rule: as-written = the 4 legally official verses (1, 2, 4, 11). as-sung = on most occasions only verse 1; on the National Holiday (1 Dec) all four official verses are sung. Default treatment: as-written = all 4 official verses; as-sung = verse 1 alone for routine occasions, with flag sung-assumed since custom varies between v1-only and the full four. Source: Wikipedia 'Deșteaptă-te, române!', nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: all 4 official verses (1, 2, 4, 11) reviewed; the 7 non-official stanzas not scored

evidence: Wake up Romanians from your sleep of death Into which you've been sunk by the barbaric tyrants.
Now or never, let us show the world That through these arms, Roman blood still flows; ... Triumphant in battles, the name of Trajan!
Behold, mighty shadows; Michael, Stephen, Corvinus ... With armed hands, with your fire in their veins, 'Life in liberty or death', they all proclaim!
Priests, lead with holy crosses, for this army is Christian, Its motto is liberty and its goal all too holy.
Better to die in battle in with eternal glory, Than to once again be slaves on our ancient lands!

anthems.title: Gosudarstvennyy gimn Rossiyskoy Federatsii (State Anthem of the Russian Federation)
anthems.sung_rule: All 3 verses + refrain are the official text and performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia 'National anthem of Russia', nationalanthems.info, Classic FM.

evidence: Russia is our holy power, Russia is our beloved country. A mighty will and great glory Are yours for all time.
Be glorious, our free Fatherland, Brotherly peoples united for centuries ... Be glorious, our country! We're proud of you!
From Arctic lands to southern seas Lie our forests and fields. Nothing like you exists in the world, Native land protected by God!
Broad spaces for dreams and for living Lie waiting for us in the years to come. Faith in our country gives us strength.

anthems.title: Inno Nazionale della Repubblica
anthems.sung_rule: Purely instrumental; no official lyrics. A Carducci text was written but never officially adopted and is not sung at ceremonies. Source: Wikipedia 'Inno Nazionale della Repubblica di San Marino', nationalanthems.info.

anthems.title: Bože pravde (God of Justice)
anthems.sung_rule: as-written = all 8 official stanzas. as-sung = stanzas 1 and 2 (usually only these are performed for brevity; the more martial stanza 3 'When our host goes forth to battle' is normally omitted). Source: Wikipedia 'Bože pravde', nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: stanzas 1-4 reviewed verbatim (covering both the sung 1-2 and the omitted martial v3); remaining official stanzas are further variations on the same God/Serb-lands petition

evidence: God of Justice; Thou who saved us when in deepest bondage cast, Hear Thy Serbian children's voices, Be our help as in the past.
God, our hope; protect and cherish, Serbian lands and Serbian race!
May the loathed fiend of discord Never in our ranks prevail. Let the golden fruits of union Our young tree of freedom grace;
Bless each Serbian town and hamlet, Mountain, meadow, hearth and spire! When our host goes forth to battle Death or victory to embrace - God of armies!
From the slough of direst slavery Serbia anew is born. Through five hundred years of durance We have knelt before Thy face

anthems.title: Nad Tatrou sa blýska (Lightning Over the Tatras)
anthems.structure: 2 official stanzas (poem has 4; only first two are the anthem)
anthems.sung_rule: Both official stanzas are sung; the 2nd stanza was added to the 1st when the anthem was adopted on Czechoslovakia's 1993 split. As-written = as-sung (both stanzas). Source: Wikipedia / classical-music.com / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Far above the Tatra lightning bolts are pounding
These bolts we shall banish, brothers, they will vanish; Slovaks are rebounding
Our Slovakia was, until now, quiescent. But the lightning flashing and the thunder crashing made it effervescent
Nad Tatrou sa blýska, Hromy divo bijú
Zastavme ich, bratia, Veď sa ony stratia, Slováci ožijú

anthems.title: Zdravljica (A Toast)
anthems.structure: 1 stanza (the 7th stanza of France Prešeren's 8-stanza poem)
anthems.sung_rule: The 1994 act on national symbols designates only the 7th stanza as the anthem; in government practice only that stanza is the anthem. As-written = as-sung (stanza 7 only). Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: the single official stanza (7th) reviewed

evidence: God's blessing on all nations, Who long and work for that bright day,
When o'er earth's habitations No war, no strife shall hold its sway;
Who long to see That all men free No more shall foes, but neighbours be.
Žive naj vsi narodi ki hrepene dočakat' dan
da rojak prost bo vsak, ne vrag, le sosed bo mejak!

anthems.title: Du gamla, Du fria (Thou Ancient, Thou Free)
anthems.structure: 2 verses (de facto anthem; 2 later verses by Louise Ahlén, 1910, are unofficial and excluded)
anthems.sung_rule: De facto anthem; the two original Dybeck verses are sung, the 1910 Ahlén verses are not official and customarily not sung. As-written = as-sung (verses 1-2). Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / Classic FM.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both customary verses reviewed; 2 unofficial Ahlén verses noted and excluded

evidence: Thou ancient, Thou free, Thou mountainous north Thou quiet, Thou joyful [and] fair!
I greet thee, loveliest land upon earth, Thy sun, Thy sky, Thy climes green.
Thou thronest on memories of great olden days, When honoured Thy name flew across the earth,
Yes, I want to live, I want to die in the North.
Du tronar på minnen från fornstora dar, då ärat Ditt namn flög över jorden.

anthems.title: Shche ne vmerla Ukrainy i slava, i volia (Ukraine Has Not Yet Perished)
anthems.structure: 1 verse + chorus (official lyrics fixed by the 2003 Law; based on Chubynskyi's 1st stanza + refrain)
anthems.sung_rule: The 6 March 2003 Law on the State Anthem made the official lyrics the first stanza + refrain only; this is what is performed at state occasions. As-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / Classic FM.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: the full official text (verse 1 + chorus) reviewed

evidence: Ukraine's freedom has not yet perished, nor has her glory,
Upon us, fellow Ukrainians, fate shall smile once more.
Our enemies will vanish like dew in the sun,
And we too shall rule, brothers, in a free land of our own.
We'll lay down our souls and bodies to attain our freedom, And we'll show that we, brothers, are of the Cossack race!
Душу й тіло ми положим за нашу свободу, І покажем, що ми, браття, козацького роду

anthems.title: Inno e Marcia Pontificale (Pontifical Anthem) — Latin lyrics
anthems.structure: single hymn text (Latin lyrics by Raffaello Lavagna, 1991; earlier Italian lyrics by Antonio Allegra, 1949)
anthems.sung_rule: The Pontifical Anthem (Gounod, 1869) is the official anthem of the Holy See / Vatican City; it does have official sung lyrics (Latin 1991, Italian 1949), so it is NOT a no-lyrics case. As-written = as-sung (single text). Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: O happy Rome - O noble Rome
You are the seat of Peter, who shed his blood in Rome, Peter, to whom the keys of the kingdom of heaven were given.
Pontiff, You are the successor of Peter; Pontiff, You are the teacher, You confirm your brethren;
Pontiff, You are the vicar of Christ on earth, a rock amidst the waves, You are a beacon in the darkness;
You are the defender of peace, You are the guardian of unity, watchful defender of liberty; in You is the authority.
Pontiff, you are the unshakable rock, and on this rock was built the Church of God.

anthems.title: Fair Antigua, We Salute Thee
anthems.sung_rule: At official events usually only the first verse is performed. As-written = all 3 verses; as-sung = verse 1 only. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Fair Antigua and Barbuda We thy sons and daughters stand Strong and firm in peace or danger, To safeguard our native land:
We commit ourselves to building A true nation, brave and free!
Ever striving, ever seeking Dwell in love and unity.
Raise the standard! Raise it boldly! Answer now to duty call: To the service of your country, Sparing nothing giving all!
Gird your loins and join the battle Against fear hate and poverty:
God of nations, let thy blessing Fall upon this land ours
Fill her fields with crops and flowers:
Never failing, all enduring To defend her liberty.

anthems.title: March On, Bahamaland
anthems.structure: single continuous song (one stanza, no separate chorus)
anthems.sung_rule: Short single-stanza anthem performed in full. As-written = as-sung (identical). Source: Wikipedia / bahamas.gov.bs / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Lift up your head to the rising sun, Bahamaland; March on to glory, your bright banners waving high.
See how the world marks the manner of your bearing! Pledge to excel through love and unity.
Pressing onward, march together to a common loftier goal; Steady sunward, tho' the weather hide the wide and treacherous shoal.
'Til the road you've trod lead unto your God, March on, Bahamaland.

anthems.title: In Plenty and In Time of Need
anthems.sung_rule: Short version (customary) = first verse + chorus; the full anthem is 2 verses + chorus. As-written = both verses + chorus; as-sung = verse 1 + chorus. Source: Wikipedia / gov.bb / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: In plenty and in time of need When this fair land was young, Our brave forefathers sowed the seed From which our pride is sprung,
A pride that makes no wanton boast Of what it has withstood, That binds our hearts from coast to coast— The pride of nationhood.
We loyal sons and daughters all Do hereby make it known These fields and hills beyond recall Are now our very own.
We write our names on history's page With expectations great, Strict guardians of our heritage, Firm craftsmen of our fate.
The Lord has been the people's guide For past three hundred years.
Upward and onward we shall go, Inspired, exulting, free, And greater will our nation grow In strength and unity.

anthems.title: Land of the Free
anthems.sung_rule: The full anthem is 2 verses + chorus; at most ceremonies the customary short performance is the first verse + chorus. As-written = both verses + chorus; as-sung = verse 1 + chorus. Source: Wikipedia / Government of Belize / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: O, Land of the Free by the Carib Sea, Our manhood we pledge to thy liberty!
No tyrants here linger, despots must flee This tranquil haven of democracy.
The blood of our sires, which hallows the sod, Brought freedom from slavery oppression's rod, By the might of truth and the grace of God.
Arise! ye sons of the Baymen's clan, Put on your armours, clear the land! Drive back the tyrants, let despots flee‐ Land of the Free by the Carib Sea!
Nature has blessed thee with wealth untold, O'er mountains and valleys where prairies roll;
Our fathers, the Baymen, valiant and bold Drove back the invader, this heritage bold
From proud Rio Hondo to old Sarstoon, Through coral isle, over blue lagoon;

anthems.title: O Canada / Ô Canada
anthems.structure: 1 official verse in each of English and French (lyrics differ; both fixed by the National Anthem Act, English line amended 2018)
anthems.sung_rule: The National Anthem Act sets official lyrics in both English and French; both single-verse versions are official and customarily performed (English in anglophone settings, French in francophone, sometimes bilingual). As-written = as-sung = the combined official English + French lyrics. Source: Canada.ca / Wikipedia.

evidence: O Canada! Our home and native land! True patriot love in all of us command.
With glowing hearts we see thee rise, The True North strong and free!
From far and wide, O Canada, we stand on guard for thee. God keep our land glorious and free!
Ô Canada! Terre de nos aïeux, Ton front est ceint de fleurons glorieux!
Car ton bras sait porter l'épée, Il sait porter la croix!
Ton histoire est une épopée Des plus brillants exploits. Et ta valeur, de foi trempée, Protégera nos foyers et nos droits.

anthems.title: Noble patria, tu hermosa bandera (Noble Homeland, Your Beautiful Flag)
anthems.sung_rule: At official ceremonies the customary performance is the chorus + the final stanza ('prodigious soil' / 'work and peace'); the chorus carries the 'rustic tool into a weapon' line. As-written = full official lyrics; as-sung = chorus + final stanza. The exact stanza partition across translations is a little ambiguous (flag sung-uncertain).

evidence: Noble homeland, your beautiful flag Gives us the expression of your life; Under the clear blue of your sky, White and pure rests peace.
In the tenacious struggle of fruitful labor That reddens man's face, Your children, simple farmhands, Conquered eternal prestige, esteem, and honor.
Hail, oh gentle land! Hail, oh mother of love! When anyone attempts to tarnish your glory, You will see your people, brave and virile, Turn the rustic tool into a weapon.
Hail, oh homeland, your prodigious soil Gives us sweet shelter and sustenance; Under the clear blue of your sky, May work and peace always live!
la tosca herramienta en arma trocar

anthems.title: La Bayamesa / El Himno de Bayamo
anthems.structure: 2 official stanzas (the original 1868 anti-Spanish song was longer; only the first two stanzas are the official anthem since 1940)
anthems.sung_rule: Officially adopted (1940) using only the first two stanzas of the longer original; both official stanzas are performed. As-written = as-sung (both official stanzas). Later anti-Spanish stanzas are non-official and excluded. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / Classic FM.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both official stanzas reviewed; later non-official stanzas noted and excluded

evidence: To combat, run, Bayamesans! For the homeland looks proudly upon you;
Do not fear a glorious death, For to die for the homeland is to live.
To live in chains is to live Mired in shame and disgrace.
Of the bugle hear the sound; To arms, brave ones, run!
¡Al combate, corred, bayameses, Que la Patria os contempla orgullosa; No temáis una muerte gloriosa, Que morir por la Patria es vivir.
Del clarín escuchad el sonido, ¡A las armas, valientes, corred!

anthems.title: Isle of Beauty, Isle of Splendour
anthems.sung_rule: All 3 verses are official and the anthem is short; whole text customarily performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: Government of Dominica portal / Wikipedia / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Isle of beauty, isle of splendour, Isle to all so sweet and fair
Rivers, valleys, hills and mountains, All these gifts we do extol.
Dominica, God hath blest thee With a clime benign and bright
And a people strong and healthy, Full of godly reverent fear.
Come ye forward, sons and daughters Of this gem beyond compare.
Strive for honour, sons and daughters, Do the right, be firm, be fair.
Toil with hearts and hands and voices. We must prosper! Sound the call
"All for Each and Each for All."

anthems.title: Himno Nacional (Quisqueyanos valientes)
anthems.structure: Original poem of 12 quatrains; the official anthem text is the first 4 quatrains
anthems.sung_rule: Official text = first 4 quatrains; at most ceremonies only the first stanza(s) are performed. as-written scored on the 4 official quatrains; as-sung scored on stanza 1 (the customarily-performed opening), flag sung-assumed as protocol detail not firmly documented. Source: nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: all 4 official quatrains reviewed; non-official remaining 8 stanzas of the original poem not scored

evidence: Brave men of Quisqueya, Let us sing with strong feeling And let us show to the world Our invincible, glorious banner.
Hail, O people who, strong and intrepid, Launched into war and went to death!
Under a warlike menace of death, You broke your chains of slavery.
No country deserves to be free If it is an indolent and servile slave
Tempered by a virile heroism.
But Quisqueya the brave and indomitable Always proudly her forehead will raise
For if she were a thousand times a slave This many times it will be free.
On to Las Carreras! Beler! ...were places Where the traces of glory are found.
Where the genius of Sanchez and Duarte Taught to be free or to die.

anthems.title: Himno Nacional de El Salvador (Saludemos la Patria orgullosos)
anthems.sung_rule: By Legislative Decree No. 342 (1992) official performance = chorus + first stanza; last two stanzas long unsung. as-written = chorus + all 3 stanzas; as-sung = chorus + stanza 1. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Let us salute the Fatherland, Proud to be called its children, And let us spiritedly swear our lives To consecrate its good without rest!
Of the peace in the supreme happiness Ever noble El Salvador dreamed
And with unwavering faith, the path of progress She strives to follow
Freedom is her dogma and her guide; A thousand times she has defended it
And as many times has she repelled The hateful power of atrocious tyranny.
Her history has been bloody and sad, Yet at the same time sublime and brilliant
A great lesson in Spartan pride.
the warlike spirit with which they have always reaped laurels of the motherland saving the honor
to waging fierce war against war: Her fortune is found in peace.

anthems.title: Hail Grenada
anthems.sung_rule: Single short stanza; whole text always sung. as-written = as-sung. Source: OAS national anthem library / Wikipedia / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Hail! Grenada, land of ours, We pledge ourselves to thee
Heads, hearts and hands in unity To reach our destiny.
Ever conscious of God, Being proud of our heritage
May we with faith and courage Aspire, build, advance As one people, one family.
God bless our nation.

anthems.title: Himno Nacional de Guatemala
anthems.structure: 4 verses, each with its own chorus (1934 softened text)
anthems.sung_rule: All 4 verses + choruses are official and sung in Guatemala. as-written = as-sung. Source: nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: all 4 verses + choruses reviewed (current 1934 official text)

evidence: Fortunate Guatemala! May your altars Never be profaned by cruel men.
May there never be slaves who submit to their yoke, Or tyrants who deride you.
If tomorrow your sacred soil Should be threatened by foreign invasion
Your fair flag, flying freely in the wind, Will call to you: Conquer or die.
From your old and hard chains You forged, with an ire-driven hand, The plow that fertilizes the soil And the sword that saves honor.
Our fathers fought one day, Lit up in patriotic burning And they were able, without bloody clash, To place you on a throne of love.
Your emblem shows a piece of the sky In which a cloud gets its whiteness Wretched is he who dares in madness stain your colors
Lying in the magnificent Andes with two oceans at hearing distance
the beautiful quetzal Native bird that lives in your seal ... hopefully he will fly high more than the condor and the royal eagle!

anthems.title: La Dessalinienne
anthems.sung_rule: All 5 verses official; first + last verse form a de facto shortened version; verse 1 is the most recognized at ceremonies. as-written = all 5 verses; as-sung defaulted to verse 1 (no firm regulation), flag sung-assumed. Source: nationalanthems.info / classical-music.com / Wikipedia.

evidence: For the Country, for the Ancestors, Let us march united, let us march united. Let there be no traitors in our ranks! Let us be the only masters of the soil.
For the Forefathers, for the Fatherland Let us toil joyous, let us toil joyous. When the field fructifies The soul fortifies.
Let us train our sons
We have a flag like all peoples. Let us love it, and die for it.
It was not a gift from the whites It was our Ancestors' blood that was shed.

anthems.title: Himno Nacional de Honduras (Tu bandera es un lampo de cielo)
anthems.sung_rule: By a late-1920s order only the chorus + seventh verse are sung at official functions and schools. as-written = chorus + all 7 verses; as-sung = chorus + verse 7. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: chorus, verse 1, verse 7 reviewed verbatim; verses 2-6 reviewed in summary (narrate Columbus, conquest, Spanish rule, independence) - no belligerent verbatim line surfaced beyond those scored

evidence: Your flag is a splendour of sky crossed with a band of snow; and there can be seen, in its sacred depths, five pale blue stars.
In your emblem, which a rough sea with its wild waves protects, behind the bare summit of a volcano, a star brightly shines.
Indian, virgin and beautiful you slept to the resounding chants of your sea
the bold navigator found you, and awe-struck at beholding your beauty
To guard this sacred emblem we shall march, oh fatherland, to our death; our death will be honoured if we die thinking of your love.
Having defended your holy flag, and shrouded in its glorious folds, many, Honduras, shall die for you, but shall fall in honour.

anthems.title: Eternal Father, Bless Our Land
anthems.sung_rule: Both verses official and short; whole text customarily performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: Jamaica Information Service / OAS national anthem library / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Eternal Father bless our land, Guard us with Thy Mighty Hand, Keep us free from evil powers, Be our light through countless hours.
To our Leaders, Great Defender, Grant true wisdom from above.
Justice, Truth be ours forever, Jamaica, Land we love.
Teach us true respect for all, Stir response to duty's call
strengthen us the weak to cherish, Give us vision lest we perish.
Knowledge send us Heavenly Father, Grant true wisdom from above.

anthems.title: Himno Nacional Mexicano (Mexicanos, al grito de guerra)
anthems.structure: Chorus + 10 stanzas (original); official sung form = chorus + stanzas 1, 5, 6, 10
anthems.sung_rule: Official/most-recognized form = chorus + stanzas 1, 5, 6, 10. as-written scored on that official form (the legally fixed performed text); as-sung identical. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: chorus + officially-sung stanzas 1, 5, 6, 10 reviewed; the 6 non-performed original stanzas not scored

evidence: Mexicans, at the cry of war, Prepare the steel and the steed
and may the earth tremble at its core to the resounding roar of the cannon.
Gird, oh Fatherland! Your brow with olive, by the divine archangel of peace
War, war without quarter to any who dare to tarnish the country's coats of arms!
War, war! Let the national banners be soaked in waves of blood.
Your countrysides will be watered with blood And in blood will be their footprints.
Your children assure to breathe until their last for your sake
For you the olive wreaths! For you a laurel of victory! For them a tomb of honor!

anthems.title: Salve a ti, Nicaragua
anthems.structure: Short - effectively a single stanza (one quatrain)
anthems.sung_rule: Short single-stanza anthem; whole text sung. as-written = as-sung. Note: deliberately a peace-themed anthem written after civil war. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Hail to thee, Nicaragua. On thy land roars the voice of the cannon no more
ya no ruge la voz del canon, ni se tine con sangre de hermanos
nor does the blood of brothers now stain thy glorious bicolour banner
May peace shine beautifully in thy sky, nothing dim thy immortal glory
for work is thy worthy laurel and honour is thy triumphal emblem!

anthems.title: Himno Istmeno (Alcanzamos por fin la victoria)
anthems.sung_rule: Chorus + both verses official; whole text performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia / lyricstranslate.

evidence: At last we reached victory In the joyous field of the union; With ardent fires of glory A new nation shines bright.
It is necessary to cover with a veil The Calvary and cross of the past
On thy soil covered with flowers, To the kisses of the warm trade wind, The warriors' clamour has ceased; Only brotherly love reigns.
Ahead the shovel and pick, At work without any more dilation
You see roaring at your feet two oceans Which give direction to your noble mission.

anthems.title: O Land of Beauty!
anthems.sung_rule: All 3 verses official and short; whole text customarily performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: nationalanthems.info / OAS national anthem library / Simple English Wikipedia.

evidence: O Land of Beauty! Our country where peace abounds, Thy children stand free On the strength of will and love.
With God in all our struggles, Saint Kitts and Nevis be A Nation bound together With a common destiny.
As stalwarts we stand, For justice and liberty, With wisdom and truth We will serve and honour thee.
No sword nor spear can conquer, For God will sure defend.
His blessings shall for ever To posterity extend.

anthems.title: Sons and Daughters of Saint Lucia
anthems.structure: 3 verses (each a six-line stanza), no separate chorus
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem; all three verses constitute the official text and are customarily sung. as-written = as-sung.

evidence: Sons and daughters of Saint Lucia, Love the land that gave us birth, Land of beaches, hills and valleys, Fairest isle of all the earth.
Gone the times when nations battled For this 'Helen of the West', Gone the days when strife and discord Dimmed her children's toil and rest.
May the Good Lord bless our island, Guard her sons from woe and harm, May our people live united, Strong in soul and strong in arm. Justice, Truth and Charity, Our ideal for ever be.

anthems.title: Saint Vincent! Land So Beautiful
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem; verses + chorus are the official text and customarily sung. as-written = as-sung.

evidence: Saint Vincent, Land so beautiful, With joyful hearts we pledge to thee Our loyalty and love, and vow To keep you ever free.
What e'er the future brings, Our faith will see us through. May peace reign from shore to shore, And God bless and keep us true.
Hairoun, Our fair and blessed Isle, Your mountains high, so clear and green, Are home to me, though I may stray, A haven, calm, serene.
Our little sister islands are Those gems, the lovely Grenadines, Upon their seas and golden sands The sunshine ever beams.

anthems.title: Forged from the Love of Liberty
anthems.sung_rule: Single-verse anthem; the whole text is sung. as-written = as-sung.

evidence: Forged from the love of liberty, In the fires of hope and prayer, With boundless faith in our Destiny, We solemnly declare,
Side by side we stand, Islands of the blue Caribbean Sea, This our Native Land, We pledge our lives to Thee,
Here every creed and race finds an equal place, And may God bless our Nation

anthems.title: Himno Nacional Argentino
anthems.structure: Original 9 stanzas + chorus; official = stanza 1 + final stanza + chorus (per 1900 decree)
anthems.sung_rule: By 1900 decree only the first verse, last verse and chorus are official and sung. as-written = those official portions; the rabidly anti-Iberian middle verses (2-8) were officially removed and are NOT scored (noted below).
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: official text (v1 + final + chorus) reviewed; omitted historical verses 2-8 reviewed for context but excluded from scoring per brief

evidence: Hear, mortals, the sacred cry: 'Freedom! Freedom! Freedom!'
Hear the sound of broken chains See noble equality enthroned.
The United Provinces of the South have now opened their very honorable throne. And the free people of the world reply: 'We salute the great people of Argentina!'
May the laurels that we knew how to obtain be eternal May we live crowned with glory Or let's swear to die with glory!
[OMITTED middle verses, not scored] 'with strong arms they tear to pieces the arrogant Iberian lion'; refers to 'vile invaders' compared to 'blood-thirsty tigers' and 'fierce tyrants'

anthems.title: Himno Nacional de Bolivia (Bolivianos, el hado propicio)
anthems.sung_rule: All 4 verses are official; in practice usually only verse 1 + chorus are sung at ceremonies. as-written = 4 verses + chorus; as-sung = verse 1 + chorus.

evidence: Bolivians, the fate of good intent, has crowned our fervent hopes and deep desire. This ground is free, our country truly free, its long enslaved condition now is gone.
The martial roar that sounded yesterday, and horrible cries of war, they now have passed.
Cherished Homeland, high and noble name, in glorious splendor, let us now preserve. And on its sacred altars, let us swear: to die rather than live on as slaves!
Eternal praise to all our brave warriors, whose heroic valor and firm resolve have won the glories that now start to bloom
Here justice raised its rightful throne on high, which vile oppression truly does not know. And in its glorious peal, was then bequeathed: Freedom! Freedom! Freedom!

If foreign power should ever one day try to subjugate Bolivia, our homeland, then let it face the fate that will befall, that threatens every proud and bold aggressor. For children of the great Bolivar have sworn a thousand times: To perish rather than see humbled low our homeland's sacred banner brought to shame.

anthems.title: Hino Nacional Brasileiro
anthems.sung_rule: Brazilian law: in vocal performances BOTH stanzas must be sung. as-written = as-sung.

evidence: The placid shores of the Ipiranga heard The resounding shout of a heroic folk And the sun of Liberty in shining beams Shone in the homeland's sky at that instant.
If the pledge of this equality We managed to conquer with strong arm, In thy bosom, O Freedom, Our chest defies death itself!
Brazil, an intense dream, a vivid ray Of love and hope descends to earth If in thy beautiful, smiling and limpid sky The image of the (Southern) Cross blazes.
Giant by thine own nature, Thou art beautiful, strong, a fearless colossus, And thy future mirrors that greatness.
Adored Land Amongst thousand others Art thou, Brazil, O beloved homeland! Of the sons of this ground Thou art kind mother, Beloved homeland, Brazil!

anthems.title: Himno Nacional de Chile (Puro, Chile, es tu cielo azulado)
anthems.sung_rule: Current official sung version = verse V + chorus only. as-written = the full 6-verse Lillo text + chorus; verse III (the 'brave soldiers' military verse) is part of the full text but only sung at military events. as-sung = verse V + chorus.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: full 6-verse text + chorus reviewed; verse III/VI martial content captured

evidence: Pure, Chile, is your blue sky; Pure breezes flow across you as well. And your flower-embroidered field Is a happy copy of Eden.
Sweet fatherland, accept the vows That were given by Chile at your altars: Either you be the tomb of the free Or the refuge against oppression
Your names, brave soldiers who have been Chile's mainstay, they are engraved in our chests; our children (verse III)
And sounding in the mouth of the strong make the tyrant always tremble (verse III)
or your noble, glorious banner will see us fighting, fall (verse VI)

anthems.title: Himno Nacional de Colombia (Oh Gloria Inmarcesible)
anthems.sung_rule: Full anthem (11 verses) rarely heard; usually performed chorus - verse I - chorus. as-written = chorus + 11 verses; as-sung = chorus + verse I.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: chorus + verse I scored from full verbatim; representative violent later verses (heroes' blood, the Andes/Junin) reviewed for as-written

evidence: O glory everlasting! O never-ending joy! Through darkness, hardship, and pain, Our virtue yet shines through!
The horrible night has ceased! (Ceso la horrible noche)
In furrows of pain, goodness now germinates.
'Independence!' shouts the American world; The land of Columbus Is bathed in heroes' blood.
Bolivar crosses the Andes watered by two oceans, swords like sparks flash in Junin.
comprehend the words of He who died on the cross.

anthems.title: Salve, Oh Patria
anthems.sung_rule: Only the second verse and the chorus (before and after) are sung. as-written = chorus + 6 verses (the omitted verse I is fiercely anti-Iberian); as-sung = chorus + verse II.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: chorus + verse II scored; verse I (anti-Spanish) reviewed for as-written

evidence: We greet you, Oh Fatherland, a thousand times! Oh Fatherland, Glory be to you!
Your breast overflows with joy and peace; And your radiant face is brighter than the shining sun we see.
The worthy sons of the soil Who magnificently adorned Pichincha, They always declared you as their sovereign lady And shed their blood for you.
God observed and accepted that sacrifice, And this blood was the prolific seed Of other heroes who amazed the world
[verse I, omitted] Indignant, your children rose against the yoke imposed by Iberian audacity ... to avenge you from the bloody monster, to break that servile yoke.

anthems.title: Dear Land of Guyana, of Rivers and Plains
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem; all 4 verses are the official text and customarily sung. as-written = as-sung.

evidence: Dear land of Guyana, of rivers and plains Made rich by the sunshine, and lush by the rains, Set gem-like and fair between mountains and sea - Your children salute you, dear land of the free.
Green land of Guyana, our heroes of yore Both bondsmen and free, laid their bones on your shore; This soil so they hallowed, and from them are we, All sons of one mother, Guyana the free.
Great land of Guyana, diverse though our strains, We are born of their sacrifice, heirs of their pains, ... One land of six peoples, united and free.
Dear land of Guyana, to you will we give Our homage, our service, each day that we live; God guard you, great Mother, and make us to be More worthy our heritage - land of the free.

anthems.title: Himno Nacional Paraguayo (Paraguayos, Republica o Muerte)
anthems.sung_rule: Usually only the first verse followed by the chorus are sung; the full text has many martial verses. as-written = full text; as-sung = verse I + chorus.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: verse I + chorus scored from verbatim; later verses (martial glory, defence) reviewed for as-written

evidence: Paraguayans: Republic or Death! Our spirit gave us liberty Neither oppressors nor slaves exist Where union and equality reign.
The peoples of the Americas, unfortunately, Were oppressed for three centuries by a scepter But one magnificent day surging forth, 'Enough!', it said, and broke the scepter.
Our fathers, grandiose in battle, Showed their martial glory; And after smashing the august diadem, Raised the triumphal cap.
A new Rome, the Fatherland shall proudly display two leaders of name and valor who, rival, like Romulus and Remus divided government and power.

anthems.title: Himno Nacional del Peru (Somos libres)
anthems.structure: Chorus + 6 original verses (anonymous verse I added later; 7 stanzas total)
anthems.sung_rule: Since 2009 the officially sanctioned sung verse is the seventh stanza ('On its summit may the Andes sustain...') + chorus, though the anonymous first verse ('long time the oppressed Peruvian') remains the popularly sung one. as-written = chorus + all verses; as-sung = official verse VII + chorus.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: chorus, verse I and official verse VII reviewed verbatim

evidence: We are free, may we always be so, and let the sun rather deny its light Than that we should be absent to the solemn vote that elevated the motherland to Eternity.
For a long time the oppressed Peruvian dragged the ominous chain; sentenced to a cruel servitude for a long time in silence he moaned. (verse I, anonymous)
On its summits may the Andes sustain The two-color flag or standard, May it announce to the centuries the effort That being free forever gave us. (verse VII, official sung)
Under its shadow let us live and swear To honor its name forever.

anthems.title: God zij met ons Suriname (God be with our Suriname) / Opo kondreman
languages_scored: English (translation from Dutch verse 1 and Sranan Tongo verse 2)
anthems.sung_rule: Both verses are official and performed in full in official performances (Dutch verse then Sranan Tongo verse). as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: God be with our Suriname, May He elevate our lovely land.
How we came here together, We are dedicated to its soil.
Justice and truth will set free, All that's good to watch, Will give value to our land.
Rise country men, rise! The soil of Suriname is calling you.
There is a fight to fight, we shall not be afraid.
God is our leader.
Our whole life until our death, We will fight for Suriname.

anthems.title: Himno Nacional del Uruguay (Orientales, la Patria o la Tumba)
anthems.structure: 11 verses + chorus (full Acuña de Figueroa text)
anthems.sung_rule: Full written text is 11 verses + chorus, but today only verse 1 and the chorus are sung at ceremonies/sport. as-written scored on the retrieved chorus + verse 1 content (later verses' verbatim lines not fully retrieved; noted). as-sung = chorus + verse 1. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: chorus + verse 1 retrieved verbatim; the remaining 10 written verses were referenced but not retrieved line-for-line (only verse 1 + chorus are sung, so as-sung is complete; as-written scored conservatively on retrieved text)

evidence: Orientals, our nation or the grave, Liberty or with glory we die.
It is the vows that our souls pronounce and which heroically we will fulfill.
Liberty, Liberty, Orientals. This is the outcry which our nation saved
and its braves in fierce battles of sublime enthusiasm enflamed
This Holy gift of Glory we deserved Tyrants: Tremble!
Liberty in combat we will Cry Out! And even dying, Freedom we shall also Shout!

anthems.title: Gloria al Bravo Pueblo (Glory to the Brave People)
anthems.sung_rule: Most performances: chorus, verse 1, chorus (or chorus alone). as-written = chorus + all 3 verses; as-sung = chorus + verse 1. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Glory to the brave people who shook off the yoke, The law respecting, virtue and honour.
"Down with chains!" Shouted the Lord; And the poor man in his hovel For Freedom implored.
Upon this holy name Trembled in great dread The vile selfishness That had once prevailed.
"Let's scream out aloud:" "Death to oppression!" Oh, loyal countrymen: Strength is unity;
And from the Empyrean The Supreme Author A sublime spirit To the people blew.
United by bonds Made by heaven, All America exists As a Nation;
And if tyranny Raises its voice, Follow the example Given by Caracas.

anthems.title: Advance Australia Fair
anthems.sung_rule: Both verses are official (adopted 1984); verse 1 is by far the most commonly sung at most occasions, full two-verse version reserved for formal state/military occasions. as-written = both verses; as-sung = verse 1. Source: Wikipedia / PM&C / Britannica.

evidence: Australians all let us rejoice, For we are one and free;
We've golden soil and wealth for toil, Our home is girt by sea.
Our land abounds in Nature's gifts Of beauty rich and rare;
In history's page, let every stage Advance Australia fair.
Beneath our radiant Southern Cross We'll toil with hearts and hands;
For those who've come across the seas We've boundless plains to share;
With courage let us all combine To Advance Australia Fair.

anthems.title: God Bless Fiji (English version) / Meda Dau Doka
languages_scored: English (the official English version; Fijian Meda Dau Doka is also official, English used far more often)
anthems.sung_rule: Both verses and chorus of the English version are performed; short hymn sung in full. as-written = as-sung. Source: nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia / Fiji Foreign Affairs PDF.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both English verses + chorus reviewed (confirmed only two verses exist)

evidence: Blessing grant, oh God of nations, on the isles of Fiji,
As we stand united under noble banner blue.
And we honour and defend the cause of freedom ever, Onward march together, God bless Fiji!
Shores of golden sand and sunshine, happiness and song.
Stand united, we of Fiji, fame and glory ever,
For Fiji, ever Fiji, let our voices ring with pride,
A land of freedom, hope and glory to endure whate'er befall May God bless Fiji, forevermore!

anthems.title: Teirake Kaini Kiribati (Stand Up, Kiribati) / Kunan Kiribati
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Stand up, People of Kiribati! Sing with jubilation!
Prepare to accept responsibility And to help each other!
Be steadfastly righteous! Love all our people!
The attainment of contentment And peace by our people Will be achieved when all our hearts beat as one,
Love one another! Promote happiness and unity!
We beseech You, O God, To protect and lead us In the days to come.
Help us with Your loving hand. Bless our Government and all our people!

anthems.title: Forever Marshall Islands (Aelon eo ao)
anthems.sung_rule: Single-verse anthem performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Source: nationalanthems.info / Wikipedia / lyricstranslate.

evidence: My island lies o'er the ocean; Like a wreath of flowers upon the sea;
With the light of the Maker from far above; Shining with the brilliance of rays of life;
Our Father's wondrous creation; Bequeathed to us, our motherland;
I'll never leave my dear home sweet home;
God of our forefathers protect and bless forever Marshall Islands.

anthems.title: Patriots of Micronesia (Across All Micronesia)
anthems.sung_rule: Short patriotic hymn (melody from 'Ich hab' mich ergeben') performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: verse 1, chorus, and a verse-3 line retrieved verbatim; full verbatim text of verses 2-3 not fully retrieved (themes scored only on retrieved lines)

evidence: 'Tis here we are pledging, with heart and with hand, Full measure of devotion to thee, our native land,
Now joined in common cause, we herewith do aspire Worldwide ones of brotherhood, our most heartfelt desire.
Now all join the chorus, let union abide... Across all Micronesia join hands on every side.
We all work together, with hearts, voice and hand, Till we have made these islands another promised land

anthems.title: Nauru Bwiema (Nauru, Our Homeland)
anthems.sung_rule: Single-verse anthem performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Nauru our homeland, the land we dearly love.
We all pray for you and we also praise your name.
Since long ago, you have been the home of our great forefathers And will be, for generations, yet to come.
We all join in together to honour your flag,
And we shall rejoice together and say: Nauru forevermore!

anthems.title: God Defend New Zealand (Aotearoa)
anthems.sung_rule: All 5 English verses are part of the official text; in practice only verse 1 (commonly with the Maori verse) is sung. as-written = all 5 verses; as-sung = verse 1. Source: Wikipedia / NZ Ministry for Culture & Heritage / Classic FM.

evidence: God of Nations at Thy feet In the bonds of love we meet, Hear our voices, we entreat, God defend our free land.
Guard Pacific's triple star From the shafts of strife and war, Make her praises heard afar, God defend New Zealand.
Men of every creed and race Gather here before Thy face, Asking Thee to bless this place, God defend our free land.
From dissension, envy, hate, And corruption guard our state, Make our country good and great, God defend New Zealand.
Peace, not war, shall be our boast, But, should foes assail our coast, Make us then a mighty host, God defend our free land.
Lord of battles in Thy might, Put our enemies to flight, Let our cause be just and right, God defend New Zealand.

anthems.title: Belau rekid (Our Palau)
