anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate / PalauGov.

evidence: Palau is coming forth with strength and power. By her old ways abides still every hour.
One country, safe, secure, one government.
Lets build our economy protecting fence. With courage, faithfulness and diligence.
Our life is anchored in Palau, our land. We with our might through life and death defend.
In spirit lets join hands, united, one. Care for our homeland from forefathers on.

anthems.title: O Arise, All You Sons
anthems.structure: single song (2 stanzas with repeated 'shout' refrain lines)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikisource / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: O arise all you sons of this land, Let us sing of our joy to be free,
Praising God and rejoicing to be Papua New Guinea.
Shout our name from the mountains to the seas Papua New Guinea;
Now give thanks to the good Lord above For His kindness, His wisdom and love
For this land of our fathers so free, Papua New Guinea.
We are independent and we're free Papua New Guinea.

anthems.title: O Le Fu'a o Le Sa'olotoga o Samoa (The Banner of Freedom / Samoa Tula'i)
anthems.structure: 2 short verses (the second largely repeats/varies the first); brief anthem performed in full
anthems.sung_rule: A short anthem; both verses are official and it is customarily performed in full, so as-written and as-sung are identical. Source: Wikipedia (The Banner of Freedom), nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Samoa, arise and raise your flag, your crown!
Look at those stars that are waving on it: This is the symbol of Jesus, who died on it for Samoa.
Oh, Samoa, hold fast your power forever.
Do not be afraid; God is our foundation, our freedom.
Samoa, arise: your flag is waving, your crown!

anthems.title: God Save Our Solomon Islands
anthems.sung_rule: Single short anthem (one verse and a closing refrain), performed in full; as-written and as-sung are identical. Source: Wikipedia (God Save Our Solomon Islands), nationalanthems.info.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: full single-verse anthem with refrain reviewed

evidence: God save our Solomon Islands from shore to shore.
Bless all her people and her Lands With Your protecting hands.
Joy, Peace, Progress and Prosperity;
That men should brothers be, make nations see.
Our Nation, Solomon Islands, Stands for evermore.

anthems.title: Ko e fasi 'o e tu'i 'o e 'Otu Tonga (Song of the King of the Tonga Islands)
anthems.sung_rule: A single-stanza anthem performed in full; as-written and as-sung are identical. Source: Wikipedia (Ko e fasi 'o e tu'i 'o e 'Otu Tonga), nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Oh almighty God above
Thou art our lord and sure defence
As your people, we trust thee, And our Tonga thou dost love
We know that thou have blessed our land
Grant our earnest supplication, God save Tupou, our king

anthems.title: Tuvalu mo te Atua (Tuvalu for the Almighty)
anthems.sung_rule: Both verses are the official lyrics; commonly the first verse is performed at ceremonies but the full text is official. Default treats as-sung = verse 1 with flag sung-assumed; as-written = both verses. Source: Wikipedia (Tuvalu mo te Atua), nationalanthems.info.

evidence: "Tuvalu for the Almighty" are the words we hold most dear;
God ever rules in heav'n above, and that we in this land are united in His love.
We build on a sure foundation when we trust in God's great law;
"May we reign with Him in glory" be our song for evermore, for His almighty power is our strength from shore to shore.
"Tuvalu free and united" be our song for evermore!

anthems.title: Yumi, Yumi, Yumi (We, We, We)
anthems.sung_rule: The chorus and verses are official; the chorus is the customarily performed core and is repeated. As-written = chorus + all verses; as-sung treated as chorus-centred performance, scores identical here since the chorus carries every theme. Source: Wikipedia (Yumi, Yumi, Yumi), nationalanthems.info.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: chorus and verses 2-3 reviewed verbatim; later verses (4-5) summarized in sources as unity/work/divine-support themes already covered

evidence: We are, we are, we are happy to proclaim We are, we are, we are the people of Vanuatu
God has given us this land, We are grateful for this,
We are strong and we are free in this land,
We are all brothers!
Many customs of before we have, Many customs from today, But we are all one,
God i helpem yumi evriwan, Hem i papa blong yumi! (God helps us all, He is our father)

anthems.title: L'Abidjanaise (The Song of Abidjan)
anthems.sung_rule: Official text is 4 verses; at ceremonies customarily only verse 1 + the refrain (verse 2, 'Fiers Ivoiriens...') are performed. As-sung scored on verse 1 + refrain; sung custom not pinned down by a single authoritative source, so flagged sung-assumed.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: all 4 official verses reviewed (French + English)

evidence: We salute you, O land of hope, Country of hospitality; Thy full gallant legions Have restored thy dignity.
Beloved Ivory Coast, thy sons, Proud builders of thy greatness, All gathered together for thy glory, In joy will we construct thee.
Tes legions remplies de vaillance Ont releve ta dignite.

Proud Ivorians, the country calls us. If we have brought back liberty peacefully, It will be our duty to be an example Of the hope promised to humanity, In building, united in the new faith The homeland of true brotherhood.
To you, noble Ivory Coast, O great country of good people! We bring you in victory The homage of our ardent hearts.
Dieu guide nous vers l'ideal (God guide us towards the ideal), Submitting to the dear motto: Union, discipline, work.

anthems.title: Djibouti (Jabuuti)
anthems.sung_rule: Very short single-stanza anthem; entire text is the official anthem and is performed in full. As-written and as-sung are identical.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: full single stanza reviewed (Somali + English)

evidence: Arise! O Flag, 'tis for thee we cheer! Thee we raise with vigour!
The Flag which cost us dear; The Flag which cleansed us of rigour.
O Flag, thy fresh green of delight, Thy sky blue and peaceful white;
'Long with thy star of our blood. O Flag, thou hast all our love.
Calankaan harraad iyo Haydaar u mudateen!

anthems.title: Bilady, Bilady, Bilady (My Homeland, My Homeland, My Homeland)
anthems.sung_rule: Officially 3 verses, but only verse 1 (framed by the chorus) is commonly/customarily sung. As-written = chorus + all 3 verses; as-sung = chorus + verse 1.

evidence: My homeland, my homeland, my homeland, My love and my heart are for thee.
Egypt! O mother of all lands, My hope and my ambition, How can one count The blessings of the Nile for mankind?
Egypt! Most precious jewel, Shining on the brow of eternity! O my homeland, be for ever free, Safe from every foe!
Egypt! Noble are thy children, Loyal, and guardians of thy soil. In war and peace We give our lives for thy sake.

anthems.title: Caminemos pisando las sendas de nuestra inmensa felicidad (Let Us Tread the Paths of Our Immense Happiness)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem built on a repeated freedom/unity chorus; performed in full. As-written and as-sung treated as identical (full text).

evidence: Let us walk treading the paths Of our immense happiness. In brotherhood, without separation, Let us sing Freedom!
After two centuries of being oppressed By colonial domination In fraternal unity, without discrimination Let's sing Freedom!
Let us shout long live free Guinea!, and let us defend our Freedom. Let us always sing free Guinea, And let us always preserve the unity.

anthems.title: Ertra, Ertra, Ertra (Eritrea, Eritrea, Eritrea)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem (prelude, two verses, chorus); performed in full at ceremonies. As-written and as-sung treated as identical.

evidence: Eritrea, Eritrea, Eritrea, Her enemy decimated, and her sacrifices vindicated by liberation.
Steadfast in her goal, symbolizing endurance, Eritrea, the pride of her oppressed people, proved that the truth prevails.
Eritrea, Eritrea, holds her rightful place in the world.
Dedication that led to liberation, Will build up and make her green, We shall honor her with progress.

anthems.title: Nkulunkulu Mnikati wetibusiso temaSwati (O Lord Our God, Bestower of Blessings of the Swazi)
anthems.sung_rule: Single short stanza, performed in full. As-written and as-sung are identical.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: full single stanza reviewed (Swazi + English)

evidence: O Lord our God, bestower of the blessings of the Swazi; We give Thee thanks for all our good fortune;
We offer thanks and praise for our king; And for our fair land, its hills and rivers;
The Blessings be on all rulers of our country; Might and power are Thine alone;
We pray Thee to grant us wisdom without deceit or malice.
Nkulunkulu Mnikati wetibusiso teMaswati

anthems.title: Wodefit Gesgeshi, Widd Innat Ityopp'ya (March Forward, Dear Mother Ethiopia)
anthems.sung_rule: Single-verse anthem, performed in full. As-written and as-sung are identical.

evidence: Respect for citizenship is strong in our Ethiopia; National pride is seen, shining from one side to another.
For peace, for justice, for the freedom of peoples, In equality and in love we stand united.
Firm of foundation, we do not dismiss humanness; We are people who live through work.
Natural grace, mother of a valorous people. We shall protect you - we have a duty; Our Ethiopia, live! And let us be proud of you!

anthems.title: La Concorde (The Concord)
anthems.sung_rule: Official text is chorus + 4 verses; customary ceremonial performance is the chorus (sung first and repeated) with verse 1. As-written = chorus + all 4 verses; as-sung = chorus + verse 1. Sung custom not authoritatively confirmed, so flagged sung-assumed.

evidence: United in concord and brotherhood, Awake, Gabon, dawn is at hand. Stir up the spirit that thrills and inspires us! At last we rise up to attain happiness.
Dazzling and proud, the sublime day dawns, Dispelling for ever injustice and shame. May it still advance and calm our fears, May it promote virtue and banish warfare.
Yes, may the happy days of which our ancestors dreamed Come for us at last, rejoicing our hearts, And banish the sorcerers, those perfidious deceivers Who sowed poison and spread fear.
So that, in the eyes of the world and of friendly nations, The immortal Gabon may maintain her good repute, Let us forget our quarrels, let us build together
From the shores of the Ocean to the heart of the forest, Let us remain vigilant, without weakness and without hatred!

anthems.title: For The Gambia Our Homeland
anthems.sung_rule: Three short stanzas, all official and performed in full. As-written and as-sung are identical.

evidence: For The Gambia, our homeland We strive and work and pray, That all may live in unity, Freedom and peace each day.
Let justice guide our actions Towards the common good, And join our diverse peoples To prove man's brotherhood.
We pledge our firm allegiance, Our promise we renew; Keep us, great God of nations, To The Gambia ever true.

anthems.title: God Bless Our Homeland Ghana
anthems.sung_rule: Three verses, all official; customarily all three are sung. As-written and as-sung treated as identical (all 3 verses).

evidence: God bless our homeland Ghana, And make our nation great and strong, Bold to defend forever The cause of Freedom and of Right.
And help us to resist oppressors' rule With all our will and might for evermore.
Steadfast to build together A nation strong in Unity; With our gifts of mind and strength of arm, Whether night or day, in the midst of storm,
In every need, whate'er the call may be, To serve thee, Ghana, now and evermore.
Raise high the flag of Ghana, And one with Africa advance; Black Star of hope and honour To all who thirst for Liberty;
Arise, arise, O sons of Ghana land, And under God march on for evermore!

anthems.title: Liberte (Liberty)
anthems.sung_rule: Short single song built on repeated 'Liberte' refrains; performed in full. As-written and as-sung treated as identical.

evidence: People of Africa! The historic past! Sing the hymn of a Guinea proud and young
Illustrious epic of our brothers Who died on the field of honour while liberating Africa!
The people of Guinea, preaching Unity, Call to Africa. Liberty!
The voice of a people Who call all her brothers of a great Africa. Let us build African Unity in a newly found independence!

anthems.title: Esta E a Nossa Patria Bem Amada (This Is Our Beloved Homeland)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem (verses + chorus), performed in full. As-written and as-sung treated as identical.

evidence: Sol, suor, o verde e o mar, Seculos de dor e esperanca; Esta e a terra dos nossos avos!
Fruto das nossas maos, Da flor do nosso sangue: Esta e a nossa patria amada. (Fruit of our hands, of the flower of our blood: this is our beloved homeland.)
Viva a patria gloriosa! Floriu nos ceus a bandeira da luta. (Long live our glorious homeland! The banner of struggle has flourished in the skies.)
Avante, contra o jogo estrangeiro! (Forward, against the foreign yoke!) Nos vamos construir Na patria imortal A paz e o progresso!
Ramos do mesmo tronco, Olhos na mesma luz: Esta e a forca da nossa uniao! (Branches of the same trunk, eyes in the same light: this is the strength of our union!)

anthems.title: Ee Mungu Nguvu Yetu (O God of All Creation)
anthems.sung_rule: All three stanzas are the official anthem (Second Schedule, Constitution of Kenya) and are sung at ceremonies; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / KLRC / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: O God of all creation / Bless this our land and nation / Justice be our shield and defender
May we dwell in unity / Peace and liberty / Plenty be found within our borders.
Let one and all arise / With hearts both strong and true / Service be our earnest endeavour
And our homeland of Kenya / Heritage of splendour / Firm may we stand to defend.
Let all with one accord / In common bond united / Build this our nation together
And the glory of Kenya / The fruit of our labour / Fill every heart with thanksgiving.

anthems.title: Lesotho Fatše La Bontata Rona (Lesotho, Land of Our Fathers)
anthems.structure: 2 verses (original had 5; only the 1st and last were adopted)
anthems.sung_rule: Both official verses (the long first stanza and the short closing stanza) are sung; as-written = as-sung. Notable: 3 of the original 5 verses were dropped and are not scored. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both official verses reviewed (3 non-official original verses excluded)

evidence: Lesotho, land of our Fathers, You are the most beautiful country of all.
You give us birth, In you we are reared And you are dear to us.
Lord, we ask You to protect Lesotho. Keep us free from conflict and tribulations.
U felise lintoa le matsoenyeho.
Oh, this land, Land of our Fathers, May it have peace.

anthems.title: All Hail, Liberia, Hail!
anthems.sung_rule: Two-verse anthem, both verses official and customarily sung; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: A home of glorious liberty, By God's command
In union strong success is sure We cannot fail!
With God above Our rights to prove We will over all prevail,
With heart and hand Our country's cause defending We'll meet the foe With valor unpretending.

anthems.title: Libiya, Libiya, Libiya (Libya, Libya, Libya / O My Country)
anthems.sung_rule: Restored 1955 anthem (re-adopted 2011). Customary ceremonial rendition is the chorus plus verse 1; verses 2 and 3 are official written text. as-sung scored on chorus + verse 1. Flag sung-assumed (exact ceremonial cut not firmly documented). Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Oh my country! With my struggle and my patience Drive off enemies and mishaps, And survive!
Survive all through, we are your ransom Oh Libya!
May God cast off any hand that would harm you Do survive! We are for ever your soldiers,
If you survive we care not who perishes.
We will never go back to fetters, we have been liberated, and we have freed our home country
Our grandfathers carried their swords when struggle called on them
Carrying the holy Quran in a hand, and the swords in the other They fought till they spread religion
O son of Libya, oh sons of wild lions We are for glory And glory is yours
And be ready for the struggle, our youth Life is a war for the glory of our home land

anthems.title: Ry Tanindrazanay malala ô! (Oh, Our Beloved Fatherland)
anthems.sung_rule: All three verses + chorus are official; the anthem is short and sung in full at ceremonies; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Oh, Our beloved fatherland / Oh good Madagascar. / Our love for you will not leave
Bless you, oh Creator / This island of our ancestors / To live in peace and joy
We wish to serve you with / The body and heart, spirit that is ours
We wish that you will be blessed, / So that the Creator of this world / Will be the foundation of your laws.

anthems.title: Mlungu dalitsani Malaŵi (O God Bless Our Land of Malawi)
anthems.sung_rule: Three official verses, sung in full at ceremonies; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: O God bless our land of Malawi, Keep it a land of peace.
Put down each and every enemy, Hunger, disease, envy.
Join together all our hearts as one, That we be free from fear. Bless our leader, each and every one
Our own Malawi, this land so fair, Fertile and brave and free. With its lakes, refreshing mountain air
Hills and valleys, soil so rich and rare Give us a bounty free.
Freedom ever, let us all unite To build up Malawi.
In time of war, or in time of peace, One purpose and one goal. Men and women serving selflessly

anthems.title: Le Mali / Pour l'Afrique et pour toi, Mali (For Africa and for You, Mali)
anthems.sung_rule: Three official verses + chorus; the first verse + chorus is the most common ceremonial rendition, but the full text is official. as-sung scored on verse 1 + chorus; flag sung-assumed. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: At your call, Mali, So that you may prosper, Faithful to your destiny, We shall all be united,
One people, one goal, one faith For a united Africa.
If the enemy should show himself Within or without, On the ramparts, We are ready to stand and die.
For Africa and for you, Mali, Our banner shall be liberty. For Africa and for you, Mali, Our fight shall be for unity.
The fields are flowering with hope And hearts are thrilling with confidence.
For the sake of our dignity Let us strengthen our ranks;
Together, shoulder to shoulder, Let us work for happiness.
Happiness through effort Will build the Mali of tomorrow.

anthems.title: Bilāda l-ʾubāti l-hudāti l-kirām (Land of the Proud, Guided by Noblemen)
anthems.structure: 6 verses + chorus (verse 5 only in extended version)
anthems.sung_rule: Adopted 2017 (Rageh Daoud). Official short ceremonial version = verse 1 (repeated) + chorus + verse 2 + verse 6. Verses 3-4 (strong enemy/resistance content) and verse 5 are written/extended only. as-written scores the full 6 verses; as-sung scores verse 1 + chorus + verse 2 + verse 6. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Land of the proud, guided by noblemen / The fortress of the Book that can't be bound
O Mauritania, the spring of harmony / The corner of tolerance, the haven of peace.
We will protect you with our lives / and paint your hills with the colour of hope
Because the noble Arabs enriched you / and the purest source is Africa's.
We made your enemy drink a bitter downpour so he couldn't settle or rest / We resist him wherever he goes
We followed the prophet along the path of the heavens to the tree of glory above the sky

anthems.title: Motherland
anthems.sung_rule: Single short stanza, sung in full; as-written = as-sung. Source: Government of Mauritius / Wikipedia / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Glory to thee, Motherland O Motherland of mine.
Sweet is thy beauty, Sweet is thy fragrance,
Around thee we gather As one people, As one nation, In peace, justice and liberty.
Beloved Country, May God bless thee For ever and ever.

anthems.title: an-Našīd aš-Šarīf (Cherifian Anthem)
anthems.sung_rule: Single official stanza (lyrics adopted 1970), sung in full; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Fountain of Freedom Source of Light Where sovereignty and safety meet,
Your champion has risen And answered your call. For your soul and your body, The victory they have conquered.
In my mouth and in my blood Your breezes have stirred both light and fire.
Up! my brethren, Strive for the highest. We call to the world That we are here ready.
We salute as our emblem God, Homeland, and King.

anthems.title: Pátria Amada (Beloved Homeland)
anthems.sung_rule: Adopted 2002. Three official verses + chorus, but customarily only verse 1 + chorus (repeated) are performed. as-written scores all 3 verses; as-sung scores verse 1 + chorus. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Beautiful fatherland of that they had dared to fight Mozambique, your name is Freedom
The Sun of June forever will shine
Mozambique, our Glorious Land Rock by rock constructing the new day Millions of arms in one only force
Joined people from Rovuma to Maputo It harvests the fruits of the combat for the Peace
Flowers sprouting of the soil of your sweat For mounts, the rivers, the sea
We swear for you, O Mozambique No tyrant in will go them to enslave

anthems.title: Namibia, Land of the Brave
anthems.structure: 1 main verse + closing refrain (single short passage)
anthems.sung_rule: Short single-passage anthem, sung in full; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / Namibian govt (MICT) / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Namibia, land of the brave Freedom fight we have won
Glory to their bravery Whose blood waters our freedom
We give our love and loyalty Together in unity
Beloved land of savannahs, Hold high the banner of liberty
Namibia our Country, Namibia Motherland, We love thee.

anthems.title: L'Honneur de la Patrie (The Honour of the Fatherland)
anthems.structure: verses + recurring 'Pour l'honneur de la Patrie' refrain, performed as one continuous piece
anthems.sung_rule: Adopted 22 June 2023, replacing La Nigerienne. Performed in full as a single continuous piece; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / Anthempedia.

evidence: From the shores of the Niger to the edges of the Tenere, Brothers and sisters we are, Children of a same fatherland Niger
For a Niger of peace, free, strong, and united, For a prosperous Niger, the country of our dreams
For the honor of the fatherland, Let us incarnate the valiance and the perseverance, And all the virtues of our worthy forefathers
Intrepid, determined and proud warriors, Let us defend the homeland at the price of our blood
Let us make Niger the symbol of dignity, The emblem and torch of Africa advancing
Forward for work and forward for the fight, We remain standing, Holding high the flag of our dear country
To construct together A world of justice, peace and progress, And to make Niger the pride of Africa
Defendons la patrie au prix de notre sang

anthems.title: Nigeria, We Hail Thee
anthems.sung_rule: Reinstated as anthem May 2024 (National Anthem Act 2024). National Orientation Agency directive: only the first stanza is sung at official events; full text is all three stanzas. as-written = all 3 stanzas; as-sung = stanza 1 only. Source: Wikipedia / Vanguard / NOA guidelines reporting.

evidence: Nigeria, we hail thee, Our own dear native land, Though tribes and tongues may differ, In brotherhood, we stand
Nigerians all, are proud to serve Our sovereign Motherland.
Our flag shall be a symbol That truth and justice reign, In peace or battle, honour'd, And this we count as gain
To hand on to our children A banner without stain.
O God of all creation, Grant this our one request, Help us to build a nation Where no man is oppressed
And so with peace and plenty Nigeria may be blessed.

anthems.title: Rwanda Nziza (Beautiful Rwanda)
anthems.sung_rule: Adopted 1 Jan 2002, replacing Rwanda Rwacu. All four verses are official; performed in full, no documented single-verse custom. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / Wikisource.

evidence: Rwanda, our beautiful and dear country, Adorned of hills, lakes, and volcanoes, Motherland, would be always filled of happiness
Let us sing your glare and proclaim your high facts, You maternal bosom of us all would be admired forever, prosperous and cover of praises.
Invaluable heritage, that God protects to you, You filled us priceless goods. Our common culture identifies us, Our single language unifies us
Our valorous ancestors Gave themselves bodies and souls As far as making you a big nation. You overcame the colonial-imperialistic yoke That has devastated Africa entirely
your joy of your sovereign independence Acquired that constantly we will defend
Standing, we commit for you So that peace reigns countrywide
that finally your pride is worth your esteem

anthems.title: Independencia Total (Total Independence)
anthems.sung_rule: Adopted 1975. Chorus + 2 verses all official; performance custom not firmly documented, so default as-sung = chorus + verse 1 (flag sung-assumed). Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Total independence, Glorious song of the people, Total independence, Sacred hymn of combat.
Dynamism In the national struggle, Eternal oath To the sovereign country of Sao Tome and Principe.
Warriors in the war without weapons, Live flame in the soul of the people, Congregating the sons of the islands Around the Immortal Fatherland.
Building, in progress and peace, With the heroic hands of the people, The happiest nation on earth.
Working, struggling, struggling and conquering, We go ahead with giant steps In the crusade of the African peoples, Raising the national flag.
To be a hero in the hour of peril, A hero of the Nation's resurgence.

anthems.title: Pincez Tous vos Koras, Frappez les Balafons / Le Lion rouge (The Red Lion)
anthems.structure: verse 1 + chorus + further verses (commonly cited as up to 5 verses)
anthems.sung_rule: Written by L. S. Senghor, adopted 1960. Verse 1 + chorus is the customary performed portion; later verses part of full text but performance custom not firmly documented, so default as-sung = verse 1 + chorus (flag sung-assumed). Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Sound, all of you, your koras, beat the balafons, The red Lion has roared, The tamer of the bush with one leap has rushed forward Scattering the gloom.
Light on our terrors, light on our hopes. Arise, brothers, behold Africa united!
Fibres of my green heart, shoulder to shoulder, O people of Senegal, more than brothers to me, arise! Unite the sea and the springs, Unite the steppe and the forest!
Hail, mother Africa, hail, mother Africa!
Restore, oh, restore to us the honour of our ancestors, Magnificent as ebony and strong as muscles! We are a straight people-the sword has no fault.
To gather the chicks, sheltering them from kites... A people rising as one, in seamless unity

anthems.title: Koste Seselwa (Join Together All Seychellois)
anthems.sung_rule: Adopted 18 June 1996. Single short verse, performed whole; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Seychelles, our only motherland Where we live in harmony Happiness, love and peace We give thanks to God.
Preserve the beauty of our country The riches of our oceans A precious heritage For the happiness of our children.
Live forever in unity Raise our flag Together for all eternity Join together all Seychellois.
Nou remersye Bon Dye

anthems.title: High We Exalt Thee, Realm of the Free
anthems.sung_rule: Adopted 1961. All 3 verses official; in practice ceremonies often perform verse 1 only. Default as-sung = verse 1 (flag sung-assumed, as it is a practice not a strict rule). Source: Wikipedia / sierra-leone.org / lyricstranslate.

evidence: High we exalt thee, realm of the free; Great is the love we have for thee; Firmly united ever we stand, Singing thy praise, O native land.
We raise up our hearts and our voices on high, The hills and the valleys re-echo our cry; Blessing and peace be ever thine own, Land that we love, our Sierra Leone.
Knowledge and truth our forefathers spread, Mighty the nations whom they led; Mighty they made thee, so too may we Show forth the good that is ever in thee.
We pledge our devotion, our strength and our might, Thy cause to defend and to stand for thy right
One with a faith that wisdom inspires, One with a zeal that never tires; Ever we seek to honour thy name, Ours is the labour, thine the fame.
We pray that no harm on thy children may fall, That blessing and peace may descend on us all

anthems.title: Qolobaa Calankeed (Every Nation Has Its Own Flag)
anthems.sung_rule: Adopted 1 Aug 2012 (composed 1959), replacing Soomaaliyeey toosoo. Short single piece performed whole; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: The flag of any nation beareth its color, but the sky, doth our flag resemble, and without defects; love it with candour!
Oh thou White Star, at thy service we are. Thou art superior, in any part of our land, be famous oh Star!
On the day thou arosest, our hearts thou hast with pureness purified! oh thou banner, Lord shan't thee dim, in this night we pray

anthems.title: South Sudan Oyee!
anthems.sung_rule: Adopted 9 July 2011 at independence; written by students/professors of Juba University. Short, performed whole; as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Oh God We praise and glorify You For Your grace on South Sudan, Land of great abundance Uphold us united in peace and harmony.
Oh Motherland We rise raising flag with the guiding star And singing songs of freedom with joy; For justice, liberty and prosperity Shall forever more reign!
Oh great patriots Let us stand up in silence and respect, Saluting our martyrs whose blood Cemented our national foundation, We vow to protect our nation.
Oh God, bless South Sudan!

anthems.title: Nahnu Jund Allah Jund Al-watan (We Are the Army of God, Army of the Land)
anthems.structure: short single piece (first four short verses of the original poem chosen at independence)
anthems.sung_rule: Words by Ahmed Mohammed Saleh, 1955; the first four (short) verses of the poem are the official anthem since 1956 independence, performed whole. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: the official short anthem (the four chosen verses) reviewed

evidence: We are soldiers of god soldiers of the homeland, if called for redemption we will not betray.
We defy death upon ordeals, we buy glory at the most expensive price.
This land is ours long live our Sudan, an edifice among the nations.
O Sons of the Sudan this is your symbol, carries the burden and protecting your land.
Nahnu Jundu'llah Jundul'watan, In Da'a Da'ie al-Fida Lan Nakhun

anthems.title: Mungu ibariki Afrika (God Bless Africa)
anthems.sung_rule: Swahili adaptation of Nkosi Sikelel' iAfrika; adopted 1961. Two verses + chorus official; performance custom not firmly documented, so default as-sung = verse 1 + chorus (flag sung-assumed). Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: God bless Africa Bless its leaders Wisdom, unity and peace These are our shields Africa and its people
Bless Africa Bless Africa Bless us, the children of Africa
God bless Tanzania Grant eternal freedom and unity To its women, men and children God bless Tanzania and its people
Mungu ibariki Tanzania Dumisha uhuru na Umoja

anthems.title: Salut a toi, pays de nos aieux / Terre de nos aieux (Land of Our Forefathers)
anthems.sung_rule: Written by Alex Casimir-Dosseh; anthem 1960-1979 and restored 1992. Three verses official; performance custom not firmly documented, so default as-sung = verse 1 (flag sung-assumed). Source: Wikipedia / lyricstranslate / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Hail to thee, land of our forefathers, Thou who made them strong, peaceful and happy, Cultivating virtue and bravery For posterity.
Even if tyrants shall come, thy heart yearns towards freedom. Togo arise! Let us struggle without faltering. Victory or death, but dignity.
God almighty, Thou alone hast made Togo prosper. People of Togo arise! Let us build the nation.
To serve thee in unity is the most burning desire of our hearts.
We the only builders of thy happiness and of thy future, Everywhere let us break chains and treachery, And we swear to thee for ever faith, love, service, untiring zeal
Everywhere, instead of misery, let us bring happiness. Let us chase from the world unruly hatred. It's over with slavery and Captivity.
Que viennent les tyrans, ton coeur soupire vers la liberte. Togo, debout! Luttons sans defaillance, Vainquons ou mourons, mais dans la dignite.

anthems.title: Humat al-Hima (حماة الحمى; Defenders of the Homeland)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem; the chorus + all three verses constitute the official text and are performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / lyricstranslate.

evidence: O defenders of the Homeland! Rally around to the glory of our time!
Blood in our veins surgeth, For the land's sake we die.
Let the heavens with thunder roar; Let thunderbolts with fire rain.
As a nation we inherited Arms like granite towers.
Arms that achieve ambitions and glory, Sure to realize our hopes, Inflict defeat on foes, Offer peace to friends.
When the folk want to live, Destiny must surely respond. Oppression shall then vanish. Fetters are certain to break.

anthems.title: Oh Uganda, Land of Beauty
anthems.sung_rule: One of the world's shortest anthems; all three verses are official and performed together. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikisource / Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Oh Uganda! May God uphold Thee, We lay our future in thy hand,
United, free, For liberty, Together we'll always stand.
Oh Uganda! The land of Freedom, Our love and labor we give,
And with neighbours' all, At our Country's call, In peace and friendship, we'll live.
Oh Uganda! The land that Feeds us, By sun and fertile soil grown,
For our own dear land, We'll always stand, The Pearl of Africa's Crown.

anthems.title: Stand and Sing of Zambia, Proud and Free (Lumbanyeni Zambia)
anthems.structure: 3 verses + chorus (chorus sung after third verse)
anthems.sung_rule: All three verses and chorus are official; full text performed. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikisource / Zambia Cabinet Office / lyricstranslate.

evidence: Stand and sing of Zambia, proud and free, Land of work and joy in unity,
Victors in the struggle for the right, We've won freedom's fight, All one, strong and free
Africa is our own motherland, Fashion'd with and blessed by God's good hand,
Let us all her people join as one, Brothers under the sun.
One land and one nation is our cry, Dignity and peace 'neath Zambia's sky, Like our noble eagle in its flight
Praise be to God, Bless our great nation, Free men we stand, Under the flag of our land

anthems.title: Simudzai Mureza WeZimbabwe (Blessed Be the Land of Zimbabwe)
anthems.sung_rule: All three verses are official. In full ceremonies the complete three-stanza anthem is performed (commonly one stanza per language, Shona/Ndebele/English); the stanzas are content-parallel, so as-written and as-sung are treated as identical. Source: Wikipedia / nationalanthems.info / Zimbabwe gov.

evidence: Oh lift high the banner, the flag of Zimbabwe, The symbol of freedom proclaiming victory;
We praise our heroes' sacrifice, And vow to keep our land from foes;
And may the Almighty protect and bless our land.
Oh lovely Zimbabwe, so wondrously adorned With mountains, and rivers cascading, flowing free;
May rain abound, and fertile fields; May we be fed, our labour blessed;
Oh God, we beseech Thee to bless our native land; The land of our fathers bestowed upon us all; From Zambezi to Limpopo May leaders be exemplary;

anthems.sung_rule: Whole anthem is short and sung in full at the twice-daily flag ceremonies. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia (Thai National Anthem), nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Thailand embraces in its bosom all people of Thai blood.
Every inch of Thailand belongs to the Thais.
It has long maintained its sovereignty, Because the Thais have always been united.
The Thai people are peace-loving, But they are no cowards at war.
They shall allow no one to rob them of their independence, Nor shall they suffer tyranny.
All Thais are ready to give up every drop of blood For the nation's safety, freedom and progress.

anthems.title: Patria ('Fatherland')
anthems.structure: Single verse with repeated opening couplet (refrain-like)
anthems.sung_rule: Short anthem performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia (Patria), nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.

evidence: Fatherland, fatherland, East Timor our Nation
Glory to the people and to the heroes of our liberation
We vanquish colonialism, we cry: Down with imperialism!
Free land, free people, No, no to exploitation.
In the struggle against imperialism, The enemy of people, until final victory, Onward to revolution.

anthems.title: Istiklal Marsi (İstiklâl Marşı, 'Independence March')
anthems.sung_rule: Full poem is official; only the first two stanzas (first ~8 lines) are recited/sung at ceremonies. as-sung scored on stanzas 1-2 only. Source: Wikipedia (Istiklal Marsi), Medio Oriente e Dintorni, Classic FM.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: full 10-stanza poem reviewed; sung portion (stanzas 1-2) identified

evidence: Fear not! The red banner that ripples in this dawn, shall fade not, Before the last hearth that is ablaze within my homeland is extinguished.
For freedom is the absolute right of my God-worshipping nation!
What madman shall put me in chains! I defy the very idea!
Frown not, I beseech you, oh you coy crescent! Smile upon my heroic nation once!
What is this violence, what for is this rage? Or our blood which we shed for you shall not be worthy afterwards…

anthems.title: Garassyz, Bitarap Turkmenistanyn Dowlet Gimni ('National Anthem of Independent, Neutral Turkmenistan')
anthems.sung_rule: 2008 official text; full text used. as-written = as-sung. Note: pre-2008 version had a third verse / 'Turkmenbasy' references, not scored. Source: Wikipedia (State Anthem of Turkmenistan), nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both verses + chorus of 2008 version reviewed

evidence: Ready I am to shed blood for our native hearth, Vigour of ancestors our sons are famous for.
'Tis the light of the neutral, independent land, The flag soars high o'er the world.
Eternal great creation of the Turkmen folk, My great stalwart state, my great sovereign land.
My nation unified in the veins of tribes, Our forebears' blood flows undyingly.

anthems.title: Ishy Bilady (عيشي بلادي, 'Long Live My Country')
anthems.sung_rule: Short single-verse anthem performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia (Ishy Bilady), Dubai Protocol Office, nationalanthems.info.

evidence: Long live my country, the unity of our Emirates lives.
You've lived for the nation's faith of Islam and guide of the Quran.
I've made you stronger in God's name, o homeland.
We have sworn to build and work – work earnestly.
The symbol of Arabism: we all sacrifice for you and give you our blood; For you we sacrifice with our souls, o homeland!

anthems.title: O'zbekiston Respublikasining Davlat Madhiyasi ('State Anthem of the Republic of Uzbekistan')
anthems.sung_rule: Both verses + chorus are official; full text used. as-written = as-sung. Source: Wikipedia (State Anthem of Uzbekistan), nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.

evidence: Stand tall, my free country, good fortune and salvation to you, You yourself a companion to friends, Oh! Loving one!
These valleys are golden - my dear Uzbekistan, Our forefathers' manly spirits your companion!
Strength of great people in turbulent times Made this land the world's joy!
Free, young generations are your mighty wings! The torch of independence, guardians of peace, Oh! Worthy motherland, flourish and prosper eternally!

anthems.title: Tien Quan Ca (Tiến Quân Ca, 'Army March')
anthems.sung_rule: Originally 2 verses; only the first verse is used in official ceremonies today. as-sung scored on verse 1. Source: Wikipedia (Tien quan ca), nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both verses reviewed; sung portion (verse 1) identified

evidence: Soldiers of Vietnam, marching onward. United in determination to save the nation, Our steps resound on the long and arduous road.
Our flag, red with the blood of victory, bears the spirit of the country.
The distant rumbling of guns mingles with our marching song.
The path to glory is built by the bodies of our foes.
Ceaselessly for the People's cause let us struggle, Let us hasten to the battlefield! Onward! All together advancing!

anthems.title: United Republic ('Raddidi', رددي, 'Repeat, O World')
anthems.structure: Chorus + 4 verses (national-anthem usage typically chorus + verse 4 + chorus)
anthems.sung_rule: Full text has chorus + 4 verses; as national anthem the customary performance is the chorus, verse 4, and chorus again. as-sung scored on chorus + verse 4. Source: Wikipedia (National anthem of Yemen), nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: chorus + all 4 verses reviewed; sung portion (chorus + verse 4) identified

evidence: Repeat, Oh World, my song. Echo it over and over again. Remember, through my joy, each martyr.
Oh my country, we are sons and grandsons of your men. We will guard all of your majesty in our hands.
Every rock on your mountains, all atoms of your soils
My banner, Oh cloth nailed from every sun Raise forever on every peak
In faith and love I am part of mankind. An Arab I am in all my life. My heart beats in tune with Yemen.

anthems.title: Fida'i (فدائي) (Warrior / Fedayeen)
anthems.sung_rule: Whole anthem (chorus + 3 verses) is official, adopted by the PLO in 1996; short and customarily performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Sources: Wikipedia, nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.

evidence: My country, my country, my land, oh land of my forefathers / Warrior, warrior, oh my people, oh people of eternity
By my strong will and my inflaming rage, my volcanic revenge
By my yearning blood for my land and home
I have climbed the mountains and combated struggles / I have subdued in the impossible and smashed the shackles
By the storming winds and the firing arms
By the oath under the shade of the flag
I will live as a Fida'i and I will remain a Fida'i, and I will die as a Fida'i until my country returns

anthems.title: Himni i Flamurit (Hymn to the Flag)
anthems.structure: official anthem = 2 stanzas (2nd functions as chorus); original poem has more stanzas (not scored)
anthems.sung_rule: Official anthem comprises the first two stanzas; usually only the first stanza is performed at events (e.g. sport). as-written = both official stanzas; as-sung = stanza 1. Sources: Wikipedia, classicfm, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both official stanzas reviewed (extra historical stanzas of the poem not scored)

evidence: Around our flag we stand united, With one wish and one goal, A sacred oath we bestow upon it Proclaiming loyalty for our salvation.
From war abstains only he, Who a traitor is born, He who is a true man is not frightened, But dies a martyr to the cause.
Na dorë armët do t'i mbajmë (We will hold the weapons in our hand) [later stanza, not in scored 2-stanza anthem]

anthems.title: El Gran Carlemany (The Great Charlemagne)
anthems.sung_rule: Single short stanza, sung in full. as-written = as-sung. Sources: Wikipedia, nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.

evidence: The great Charlemagne, my father, from the Saracens liberated me
And from heaven he gave me life, of Meritxell, the great Mother.
Princess, born Heiress Into two nations, in neutrality
I am the only remaining daughter, of the Carolingian empire.
Faithful and free for eleven centuries, Faithful and free I will be.
The laws of the land be my tutors, and my Princes defenders!

anthems.title: Land der Berge, Land am Strome (Land of Mountains, Land by the River)
anthems.sung_rule: All 3 stanzas official; commonly the first stanza is performed but the full text is official. as-written = 3 stanzas; as-sung = stanza 1 (customary, flagged assumed). Sources: Wikipedia/germanhistory-intersections, classical-music, lyricstranslate.

evidence: Land of mountains, land by the river, Land of fields, land of cathedrals, Land of hammers, with a promising future!
Home to great daughters and sons, A nation highly blessed with beauty, Much-praised Austria
Strongly feuded for, fiercely hard-fought for, You lie in the middle of the continent Like a strong heart
Since the early days of the ancestors thou hast Borne the burden of a high mission, Much-tried Austria
Bravely towards the new ages See us striding, free, and faithful, Assiduous and full of hope
Unified, let us in jolly choirs Pledge allegiance to thee, Homeland, Much-beloved Austria

anthems.title: My, bielarusy (Мы, беларусы) (We Belarusians)
anthems.sung_rule: Full text (3 verses + chorus) official since 2002 decree; performed in full. as-written = as-sung. Sources: Wikipedia, nationalanthems.info, en.wikisource, lyricstranslate.

evidence: We, the Belarusians, peaceful people, Our heart is to our native land
We maintain generous friendship and gain our powers Within the industrious, free family.
Glory to the bright name of our land, Glory to the fraternal union of our peoples!
Our beloved mother-Motherland, Long you live and flourish, Belarus!
Together with brothers, with fortitude, during centuries We guarded our native thresholds
In struggles for freedom, in struggles for fate, We have been gaining our banner of victories.
The friendship of peoples (which is) the power of peoples Is our venerable, sunny path

anthems.title: La Brabançonne (The Brabantian)
anthems.structure: original poem several verses; only the 4th verse (Charles Rogier text) is official since a 1921 ministerial circular
anthems.sung_rule: 1921 circular decreed only the 4th verse official in all three languages; this is the verse used at ceremonies. as-written = official 4th verse; as-sung = same 4th verse. (A modern unofficial trilingual verse is sung on National Day but is not official.) Sources: Wikipedia, classicfm, nationalanthems.info.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: the single official (4th) verse reviewed; non-official earlier verses not scored

evidence: O beloved Belgium, sacred land of our fathers, Our heart and soul are dedicated to you.
Our strength and the blood of our veins we offer, Be our goal, in work and battle.
O land, in unity unbreakable Be always yourself, unsubjugated
Your motto be immortally spoken: For King, for Freedom, and for Right.

anthems.title: Državna himna Bosne i Hercegovine ("Intermeco")
anthems.sung_rule: Instrumental anthem (composed by Dušan Šestić, adopted 1999/2001). No official lyrics have ever been adopted; multiple attempts (latest 2018) failed over political disagreement. Source: Wikipedia, nationalanthems.info, worldatlas.

anthems.title: Mila Rodino (Мила Родино) (Dear Motherland)
anthems.structure: official since 1991 = verse 1 + chorus; communist-era fighting/Soviet verses removed (not scored)
anthems.sung_rule: On 12 July 1991 the anthem was shortened to its first verse + chorus, which is the official and sung form. as-written = as-sung = verse 1 + chorus. The extra 'countless fighters fell' verses are former communist-era additions, removed; not scored. Sources: Wikipedia, nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: official verse 1 + chorus reviewed (historical communist verses noted but not scored)

evidence: Proud Balkan mountains, next to them the Danube flows, the sun sheds its light over Thrace, shining over Pirin.
Dear native land, you are paradise on earth, your beauty and your charm, ah, they never end.
[Historical communist-era verse, not in current official anthem:] Countless fighters fell for our beloved people, mother, give us manly strength to continue their path

anthems.title: Lijepa naša domovino (Our Beautiful Homeland)
anthems.structure: official anthem = 4 stanzas; original poem had 14 stanzas (rest not scored)
anthems.sung_rule: Official anthem is the 4-stanza version; on most occasions only the first stanza is performed. as-written = 4 stanzas; as-sung = stanza 1. Sources: Wikipedia, nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: all 4 official stanzas reviewed (original 14-verse poem not fully scored)

evidence: Beautiful is our homeland, O so fearless, o so gracious, Our fathers' ancient glory, May God bless you, live forever!
You are our only glory, You are our only treasure, Yes, we love your plains and valleys, Yes, we love your hills and mountains.
Sava, Drava, keep on flowing, Danube, do not lose your vigor, Deep blue sea go tell the whole world, That a Croat loves his homeland.
When his fields are kissed by sunshine, When his oaks are whipped by wild winds, When his dear ones go to heaven, Still his heart beats for Croatia!
Dok mu mrtve grobak krije (When his dead are hidden by the grave)

anthems.title: Ýmnos is tin Eleftherían (Ὕμνος εἰς τὴν Ἐλευθερίαν) (Hymn to Liberty)
anthems.structure: official anthem = first 2 stanzas of Solomos's 158-stanza poem (same anthem as Greece)
anthems.sung_rule: Cyprus adopted the Greek 'Hymn to Liberty' in 1966; official = first two stanzas, performed as such. as-written = first 2 stanzas; as-sung = same 2 stanzas. Sources: Wikipedia, nationalanthems.info, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: both official stanzas reviewed (remaining 156 poem stanzas not scored)

evidence: I shall always recognise you by the dreadful sword you hold
I know you by the dreadful edge of the sword, I know you by the gaze that measures the land with force.
Born from the sacred bones of the Greeks, and brave as in the old times, Hail, oh hail, Liberty!
From the graves of our slain Shall thy valour prevail (Kipling rendering)
'Twas the Greeks of old whose dying brought to birth our spirit free. Now, with ancient valour rising, Let us hail you, oh Liberty!

anthems.title: Kde domov můj (Where Is My Home)
anthems.structure: official anthem = first stanza only (per Czech Act No. 3/1993); original song had 2 stanzas
anthems.sung_rule: Czech law (Act 3/1993, amended 154/1998) designates only the first stanza as the official anthem; performed as such. as-written = as-sung = stanza 1. The second stanza is not part of the official anthem and is not scored. Sources: Wikipedia, nationalanthems.info, classical-music.
anthems.completeness: COMPLETE: official first stanza reviewed (non-official 2nd stanza not scored)

evidence: Where my home is, where my home is, Streams are rushing through the meadows
Midst the rocks sigh fragrant pine groves, Orchards decked in spring's array
Scenes of paradise portray. And this land of wondrous beauty, Is the Czech land of my home

anthems.title: Der er et yndigt land (There Is a Lovely Land)
anthems.structure: original poem 12 verses; the anthem as used = verses 1, 3, 5 and the last; at events only stanza 1 + last lines of v.4 customarily sung
anthems.sung_rule: Anthem comprises the first, third, fifth and last verses; at sporting events only the first stanza (plus the last lines of verse 4) is sung. as-written = those official verses; as-sung = stanza 1 (with the closing lines), so monarch line of the final verse mostly drops out of the sung text. Verse 2 (armoured Vikings / bloody frays) is NOT part of the anthem and is not scored. Sources: Wikipedia, nationalanthems.info, classicfm, lyricstranslate.
anthems.completeness: PARTIAL: verses 1, 3 and last reviewed verbatim; verse 5 text not located in searches (noted). Non-official verse 2 excluded.

evidence: I know a lovely land With spreading, shady beeches Near Baltic's salty strand; Its hills and valleys gently fall, Its ancient name is Denmark, And it is Freya's hall
This land is still as fair, The sea is blue around it, And peace is cherished there.
Strong men and noble women still Uphold their country's honour With faithfulness and skill
Hail King and Fatherland! Hail every Danish person For doing what he can
The Viking land for Danes is true, With fields and waving beeches By a sea so blue
[Non-official verse 2, not scored:] There in the ancient days The armoured vikings rested Between their bloody frays
